## Threat & Motivations

[[Kyusse Nihal]]'s most documented ability is its capacity to facilitate the manifestation of [[star spawn]] in its vicinity. The entity essentially lowers the barriers between realities, making it easier for these aberrant creatures to enter the material plane. This constitutes a significant threat to the stability of [[Neverwinter]] and potentially the wider region.

The party suspects [[Kyusse Nihal]] may be actively undermining the gods of death and their natural order. If true, this suggests the entity operates against divine authority and the fundamental balance of life, death, and the afterlife. This motivation connects to apparent cult activity within [[Neverwinter]], implying organized worship or servitude to the aberrant being.

This assault demonstrated Kyusse's ability to project power across distance and manifest alien, tentacled creatures in addition to traditional undead forces.

During the investigation into the plague, the Archdruid made clear his conviction that evil worshippers of [[Kyusse]] and [[Zugtamoid]] operating within [[Neverwinter]] are responsible for the outbreak. He identified a corrupted fungus called [[aromycos]] as central to the plague's origin and spread. He possesses [[agate]] and indicated he may be capable of helping the party obtain [[diamonds]] needed for resurrection magic, suggesting his grove contains valuable resources or connections to acquire them.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The manor became the party's home and served as a gathering point during their investigations into the plague affecting [[Neverwinter]]. It was here that party members received lodging and established their individual spaces. The manor has hosted numerous strategy sessions where the group recapped their findings and coordinated next steps in their investigations. The graves in the rear grounds serve as a solemn reminder of losses connected to the party's adventures.

retrieved.heading: Role in the Campaign
retrieved.document:
## Role in the Campaign

The Archdruid serves as a potential ally in the party's [[Plague Investigation]]. His knowledge of unnatural corruption and connection to the land make him a valuable resource for understanding the origins and nature of the plague affecting [[Neverwinter]]. He may provide assistance in acquiring rare materials, particularly diamonds, which could be essential for resurrection rituals. His access to the Druid Grove and its resources positions him as someone who can offer both practical aid and arcane knowledge.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Aarlon was imprisoned following an assassination attempt but negotiated his release by accepting a 30-day Geas with the binding instruction to "Serve Neverwinter." This magical compulsion causes him physical pain when violated, creating a complicated constraint on his actions. He has been actively surveilling members of the [[Council of Neverwinter]] and uncovered evidence that a corrupt counselor hired the [[Tarrith]] to monitor [[Lord Neverember]]. During the plague investigation, Aarlon coordinated emergency resupply efforts for armed forces and revealed that a [[Drow]] was responsible for burning down [[My Chemical Reagents]], while also disclosing that [[Nym]], brother of [[Tarrith]], was staying at the [[Beach Leviathan]].

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party first became aware of Neverember's potential involvement in the plague investigation when his official seals (or convincing forgeries) were discovered on crates at [[My Chemical Reagents]] containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders given to the [[Tarrith]], though the party later learned Tarrith was not directly employed by him and received instructions via dead drops—suggesting his seal may have been forged or misused.

By Session 35-36, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]], privately confessing the city's desperate state while maintaining a facade of authority. He reconciled with [[Arlon]] and revealed that [[Edgar]] died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate. Most significantly, in Session 37, he convened faction representatives at [[Castle Never]] and delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority, pledging to personally accompany them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]].

retrieved.heading: [[Aust]] exploring outside the city
retrieved.document:
## [[Aust]] exploring outside the city

- [[Aust]] looked around the [[House of Knowledge]]. It is open but quiet.
- [[Aust]] goes to leave [[Neverwinter]] by the exit closest to the [[Druid Grove]]. The gate is closed and guarded. Things periodically crash into the protective shield (it seems otherwise invisible?) which is around 30/40ft beyond the gate. The weather is terrible - horizontal hail. [[Aust]] stealths out using pass without trace.
- [[Aust]] headed into the [[Druid Grove]] and found an alien humanoid creature with too many appendages & kaleidoscope eyes being held aloft and being squeezed by tree branches.  There is the stench of death on the wind, something non-human or animal is decomposing. There is no sign of the druids.
	- two branches of the great tree are holding the creature - the tree has been awakened. The alien's skin is decaying, so likely to have been here a few days. It appears dead.
- There are almost no creatures in the grove, but there is a spider a squirrel and a bird. As [[Aust]] enters the grove a tree waves to him. The spider comes across to him and turns into a human - the [[Archdruid]], who stares at [[Aust]] with glazed eyes.
	- He tells [[Aust]] that the grove was attacked but is fine now. He doesn't like the magic shield around the city. He knows about the #plague and that the cure is to kill is source, which he says says was the heavens/their messengers.
	- The alien is one of the many [[Starspawn of Nihal]]. Blunt force and magic kills them.
	- The [[Archdruid]] speculates that their is darkness in the city which causes their #plague.
- The fungal plague is catastrophic, caused by an evil fungus. The [[aromycos]] has been turned evil and the [[Archdruid]] is unsure how to turn the fungus back. The mushrooms only repeat "She is wed, she is beautiful."
- [[Aust]] is given some Agate by the [[Archdruid]] and is on the hunt for a diamond. [[Aust]] tells him that he was fighting as par of the [[Sharn]] and of the fight in the [[Underdark]]. 
- The [[Archdruid]] is convinced that its the end of the world. He suggests that we kill all the worshippers of [[Kyusse]] and [[Zugtamoid]] who must be within [[Neverwinter]], driving the #plague. If we can sever the link to the [[aromycos]] then [[Zugtamoid]] could be vulnerable.
- [[Aust]]  tells the [[Archdruid]] that he needs diamonds to resurrect his mother. The [[Archdruid]] says he possible could help but wants to know how she could help.
	- [[Aust]] tells him that she used to be a protector of the forest. He thinks she would have unfinished business.
	- The [[Archdruid]] tries to find rare diamonds but is thrown off by the presence of [[Neverwinter]] and suggests looking further away from the city.
- [[Aust]] tries to get back through the force field around [[Neverwinter]] by walking through it, but finds it is solid. He tries digging under it but it looks like the shield keeps going. He then walks to the gate and is watched by guards shouting at him, which he cant hear. 
	- [[Aust]] sits outside the gate until a creature smashes into the dome, seeming to cause a moment of weakness, at which point he runs through.

retrieved.heading: Role in the Campaign
retrieved.document:
## Role in the Campaign

The High Inquisitor has been deeply involved in investigating and combating the plague that devastated [[Neverwinter]]. During the height of the crisis, he conducted forbidden magical research into the nature of plague-infected undead, using divination magic to read the thoughts of these creatures. He maintains or maintained a secret underground chamber beneath the [[Neverwinter Temple of the Raven Queen]] containing alchemical reagents and divination materials necessary for this work.

More recently, he was posted to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]], where he may have overseen funerary rites. His involvement in death magic rituals at the graveyard has raised concerns that he was either corrupted or deceived into enabling the corrupted death magic now affecting that location.

retrieved.heading: Threat & Motivations
retrieved.document:
## Threat & Motivations

Zugtamoid poses a multi-layered threat to [[Neverwinter]] and the surrounding region. Its primary vector of influence is through [[Araumycos]], which it has infected and now controls through dark tendrils that constrict and dominate the fungal entity's consciousness. This relationship is described as intimate and choking—suggesting Zugtamoid's corruption is not merely parasitic but deeply invasive. Through this control, Zugtamoid perpetuates the plague affecting [[Neverwinter]], spreading fungal spores throughout the city.

The entity's motivations appear tied to expansion and corruption rather than destruction alone. While other threats may pose more immediate danger, Zugtamoid's slow but methodical spread through the Araumycos represents a persistent existential threat. The corruption manifests as rot and fungal degradation, fundamentally altering the nature of whatever it infects.

content.text: I'll search the campaign notes for information about Kyusse and the plague in Neverwinter.

Based on the campaign notes, here's what the party knows about Kyusse and the plague in Neverwinter:

## About Kyusse

**Identity & Nature:**
- Kyusse is a powerful necromancer who has discovered methods to achieve ascension through dark magic and undeath
- Kyusse operates from **Wormcrawl Island** off the coast of Chult, commanding a cult of reanimated followers and mysterious worm-spawn creatures
- The House of Knowledge actively suppresses information about Kyusse among the wizarding community to prevent other mages from attempting similar necromantic ascensions

**Capabilities:**
- Controls undeath and commands both reanimated cultists and alien worm-spawn creatures
- Can manifest **star spawn** in their vicinity by lowering barriers between realities
- Demonstrated ability to project power across distance (worm-spawn creatures emerged during a ritual battle at sea)
- The party suspects Kyusse may be undermining the gods of death and their natural order

**Motivation:**
- Seeks godhood or transcendence through necromantic transformation and accumulated power

## Connection to the Plague

**The Archdruid's Investigation:**
- The Archdruid identified Kyusse's cult as the driving force behind the plague ravaging Neverwinter
- Evil worshippers of Kyusse (and Zugtamoid) operating within Neverwinter are responsible for the outbreak
- The corrupted **aromycos fungus** (a natural element) has been weaponized to spread disease

**The Plague's Impact:**
- Killed approximately 100 people nightly at its height
- The Neverdeath Graveyard maintained massive pyres to handle the dead
- After the party's return from the Far Realm, incomprehensible entities breached the city's defenses, causing massive damage to Castle Never's western wall

**Party Objectives:**
- Stop the plague by finding and eliminating the worshippers of Kyusse and Zugtamoid within the city
- Deal with Wormcrawl Island (the unmapped island where Kyusse ascended) to find information about Kyusse

<thinking>I have found information about Ultrin and its importance to Nand and the Neverwinter barrier. I will summarize the key points to answer the user's question.</thinking>

Ultrin is important to both Nand and the Neverwinter barrier for several reasons:

1. **Nand's Sacrifice**: In Session 34, Nand sacrificed himself during a ritual against worms at a ship's prow. He activated a spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to Ultrin before collapsing. This act fundamentally weakened Nand and led to his disappearance while fighting a powerful star spawn.

2. **Creation of the Barrier**: The barrier currently protecting Neverwinter was created by Nand's spell. This means that the Neverwinter Academy is dependent on Nand's continued presence and guidance.

3. **Ultrin's Role in the Campaign**: Ultrin played a crucial role in the climactic events of the Neverwinter arc. He orchestrated the final ritual against the worm star, persuading the party to participate by framing their involvement as an expression of fate and divine will. His command of forbidden magical knowledge and ability to wield reality-bending forces made him indispensable to confronting the cosmic threat.

4. **Corruption and Destabilization**: Following Nand's ritual sacrifice, the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon Ultrin, transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

5. **Campaign Involvement**: The book played a critical role in resolving the worm infestation threatening Neverwinter. Its intervention proved decisive in both combating the immediate threat and affecting celestial bodies, demonstrating power at scales far beyond typical magic.

In summary, Ultrin is important to both Nand and the Neverwinter barrier because of Nand's sacrifice, the creation of the barrier, Ultrin's role in the campaign, the corruption and destabilization of the Weave, and the book's involvement in the campaign.

## Key Events

Nand's most critical act was sacrificing himself during a ritual against worms at a ship's prow in Session 34. He activated a spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to [[Ultrin]] before collapsing, fundamentally weakening himself in the process. Shortly thereafter, he disappeared while fighting a powerful star spawn, and his body was never recovered. His death triggered formal mourning throughout [[Neverwinter]], though investigations subsequently revealed he had committed crimes. The barrier currently protecting [[Neverwinter]] was created by Nand's spell, leaving the [[Neverwinter Academy]] dependent on his continued presence and guidance.

He orchestrated the final ritual against the worm star, persuading the party to participate by framing their involvement as an expression of fate and divine will. His command of forbidden magical knowledge and ability to wield reality-bending forces made him indispensable to confronting the cosmic threat. retrieved.heading: Corruption and Destabilization
retrieved.document:
## Corruption and Destabilization

[[Nihal]]'s solar flare pulses are actively consuming the Weave, threatening to unravel all magical structure and cause the gods themselves to fade from existence. This consumption has rendered the Weave vulnerable to external manipulation and has fundamentally weakened magic throughout reality.

Following [[Nand]]'s ritual sacrifice during the events at [[Neverwinter]], the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon [[Ultrin]], transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

The consequences have been severe: magic throughout the world is now significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to [[Neverwinter Academy]] experiences particularly volatile magical instability, making the Weave unpredictable and dangerous to those who channel it.

The book played a critical role in resolving the worm infestation threatening [[Neverwinter]]. Its intervention proved decisive in both combating the immediate threat and affecting celestial bodies, demonstrating power at scales far beyond typical magic. The circumstances surrounding how [[Ultrin]] obtained the book and his connection to it remain unclear, suggesting deeper mysteries yet to be uncovered.

answer:
Based on the campaign notes, here's why Ultrin is so important to both Nand and the Neverwinter barrier:

## Ultrin's Critical Importance

**Connection to Nand:**
In Session 34, Nand sacrificed himself during a ritual against the worms at a ship's prow. Before collapsing, Nand transferred incomprehensible weave-power directly to **Ultrin**, making him the material component of the spell. This wasn't incidental—Nand specifically entrusted everything to Ultrin, recognizing something in him that made him essential to the ritual's success.

**Connection to the Neverwinter Barrier:**
The barrier protecting Neverwinter was created by Nand's spell, but its continued function is fundamentally dependent on Ultrin. Here's why:

1. **Ultrin absorbed The Weave itself**: During Nand's ritual, an incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon Ultrin. The threads of The Weave became tangible and condensed permanently into him, granting him a direct connection to magical reality itself.

2. **He's the anchor point**: The barrier's integrity is tied to this connection. Magister Eloin Vale explicitly tells Ultrin in Session 38 that "only after [[Nand|Archmage Nandntiersanon]]'s spell did it become possible" to manipulate the barrier as it currently is, and that **Ultrin was the material component of the spell**—meaning the barrier's very existence is bound to him.

3. **He can perceive and influence The Weave**: Ultrin can see The Weave in the same way others see light, allowing him to understand how Nand changed it and how it's being manipulated. This makes him uniquely positioned to maintain, repair, or modify the barrier.

In essence, Ultrin is the living conduit through which Nand's protective magic flows—without him, the barrier that shields Neverwinter from the Starspawn of Nihal would likely fail.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

The Neverwinter barrier is a magical protective enchantment surrounding the city of [[Neverwinter]], designed to shield it from external threats. Currently, the barrier is under sustained assault by the [[Starspawn of Nihal]], an otherworldly force besieging the city. The barrier's integrity is a critical concern for the city's survival, as breaches or weakening could allow these hostile entities to penetrate the defenses and wreak devastation within.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Nand's most critical act was sacrificing himself during a ritual against worms at a ship's prow in Session 34. He activated a spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to [[Ultrin]] before collapsing, fundamentally weakening himself in the process. Shortly thereafter, he disappeared while fighting a powerful star spawn, and his body was never recovered. His death triggered formal mourning throughout [[Neverwinter]], though investigations subsequently revealed he had committed crimes. The barrier currently protecting [[Neverwinter]] was created by Nand's spell, leaving the [[Neverwinter Academy]] dependent on his continued presence and guidance.

retrieved.document:
## At the [[Neverwinter Academy]]

- the path there is unsettling - magic has been stretched to achieve this and is making reality unstable. [[The Weave]] here feels wrong - volatile. There is also a feeling like the tunnel (the "umbilical)" is reacting to their presence, as if there was some gravitational pull and every step causes it to extend and resize. 
	- Magical runes within the barrier light up as they walk past, seeming to make the barrier stronger.
	- [[Aust]] feels a bit weird, but doesn't seem to have the same reaction. He feels like he is walking over roots and plants with every step. [[Ultrin]] swears that he can hear the sound of pages turning with every step.
- Eventually they reach [[Neverwinter Academy]]  and things feel more normal. They can hear the bells tolling and the sound of boots on stone as well as shouting.
	- Gathered in the courtyard is a crowd/mob, arguing with each other and focused on someone at the centre of the crowd. A man in robes appears to be standing on something, wearing dishevelled robes - this is a lecturer called [[Master Alevar]] (a lecturer in the planes and the history of the planes. Found of telling horror stories about the planes to *educate* people).
	- [[Aust]] approaches the crowd. [[Ultrin]] announces his return to the nearest of those in the crowd, they don't care but suggests he stops [[Master Alevar]], who is very riled up
	- [[Master Alevar]] declaims "We gather under these towers as a shelter, but they are built on magic. After the spell plague, schools were built around it. But now the heavens bleed and worms answer spells. Yet people ask the wizards to save you from the horrors that they caused. [[Nand]] is called a hero for bending the world but failing to break it. Perhaps breaking the world would be a mercy. Perhaps cauterising the wound would be best. Would a lich survive the death of magic? Would the [[Starspawn of Nihal]] survive without [[The Weave]] to feed upon? Salvation will come if we stop worshipping the thing that gave birth to this evil."
	- Some seem deeply offended by this, others think he has a point and that wizards *are* the bad guys. A student screams that he's a traitor and the guards move into the crowd (reluctantly), intent on breaking up the situation. They seize [[Master Alevar]], who doesn't fight, but does spot [[Ultrin]], displays no real emotions.
- [[Ultrin]] jumps up onto the vacated platform. He is immediately heckled, both positively and negatively. [[Ultrin]] urges the airing of all views, but in discussion, which [[Ultrin]] promises he will ensure happens. 
	- Some people like this, others hate the idea.

- [ ] Find a way to stop the groups worshipping Nihal
- At HoK
	- [x] Blueprints for the graveyard
	- [ ] tunnels in/to graveyard
	- [ ] How/if disrupt bad rituals
	- [ ] Start the funeral ASAP (are the bells hurting us?)
	- [ ] Stop destroying stuff
	- [ ] Find [[Jarick]]
- At NA
	- [ ] Magic items to help us?
	- [ ] Seeds
	- [ ] Can reshape the shield to cover graveyard? (briefly) & weird portable shield item
	- [ ] Politics

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The barrier has a history of catastrophic failure. It has shrunk during previous crises, with each contraction resulting in significant loss of life among Neverwinter's inhabitants. This traumatic precedent weighs heavily on current defensive strategies.

A concerning development has emerged: a proposed protective spell device has been identified that could theoretically strengthen defenses, but analysis suggests it might instead weaken the barrier. If implemented, this device could potentially create openings large enough to allow the [[Starspawn of Nihal]] to breach the city's primary defense. The risk of deploying untested magical solutions during active siege conditions remains dangerously high, forcing difficult decisions about Neverwinter's protection strategy.

## 🎒 Items & Loot

- [[Dawnbringer]] — [[Jorund]] — feels diminished, "more of a torch than a bright light, as if not whole"; now feels like an ally with a shared goal rather than a friend
- [[Frost brand scimitar]] ([[Jungo]]'s sword) — [[Merric]] — no longer sheds light; ability to suppress the cold cannot be toggled; emits feelings of pity and loss as if carrying the emotions of [[Merric]]'s clan
- [[Figurine of wondrous power]] — [[Merric]] — now appears inert
- [[Orb of the Vale]] — [[Jorund]] — now causes nervous unease rather than reassurance
- [[Amulet of the Devout]] — [[Jorund]] — appears newly "polished"
- [[Crystal ball]] — [[Ultrin]] — missing from room on arrival; [[Ultrin]] feels drawn toward his bookcase instead
- [[Amulet of the Plains]] — [[Ultrin]] — missing from room on arrival

---

# [[Neverwinter]]
- we are pulled out of the river, surrounded in lantern light. It is late evening/night. We hear gruff voices of the people hauling us in - many are wearing holy symbols of swirling waves - they seem amused at the situation.
	- one of them whispers "it's them, they've come back, that's it then". All of the men step back, several holding their amulets.
	- there are still small flowers sprouting from [[Aust]]'s feet, but the sprouts wither with illness
	- [[Jorund]] is holding [[Dawnbringer]] and feels this was all a memory, not just a vision. It seems that [[Dawnbringer]] is more of a torch than a bright light, as if not whole. Always hard to interpret, [[Dawnbringer]] now seems to not be friends, but allies with a shared goal. [[Jorund]]/[[Dawnbringer]] is sad at the fear of the men around them and wants to prove they aren't the enemy
	- [[Merric]] scans the crowd and sees hardworking commoners (the fishermen) who seem scared of everything, muttering prayers for a range of things. Some are scared that the gods spat us back out, some resentful that heroes live on, all seem scared/angry. It feels like the desperate old beliefs of home and are focusing their fear on the wrong thing. [[Merric]] feels a sense of pity and loss from [[Jungo]]'s sword, as if the emotions of his clan.
	- [[Ultrin]] feels nothing for these people, they are unimportant. The weave has been disturbed. The barrier around the city is no longer a sphere, is now an oval. Something is distorting thee shape and forcing the weave to act in a way it shouldn't. The idea of abusing [[The Weave]] is scary, but could be turned to great power.
	- [[Ultrin]] gets the sense these people don't know what to do, they seem panicked, deciding between bad options of attack or run
	- [[Aust]] and [[Merric]] notice that someone had left the back of the crowd and is jogging towards the city. This gives the feeling of being discovered. The two of us run after the fleeing figure, the people try to block our way but don't stand their ground. [[Ultrin]] casts command on the crowd and the crowd. [[Merric]] shouts at the crowd to get out of the way (and the mostly do)
	- [[Jorund]] persuades the majority for people that they would calm down and that were friends
	- The man running towards town suddenly stops, he is carrying a lantern, seeing a figure appear at the edge of his lantern light. It is a clergyman in dark robes with a silver feather in the clasp of the robes,  marking him as a [[Raven Queen]] follower. The man seems respectful of the clergyman, without ceremony. [[Aust]] and [[Merric]] catch up
	- [[Ultrin]] and [[Jorund]] see the crowd quiet down at the appearance of the clergyman. He appears fixated on [[Jorund]]  for a moment he seems to look over [[Merric]]'s shoulder and says "oh you walk adjacent". [[Merric]] demands to know what that means: "my queen values those who break that which should be broken. You will not be opposed"
	- [[Aust]] introduces himself to the fisherman, who says his name is [[Phil]]. [[Merric]] backs up [[Aust]] asking him what he was doing to do, he tells us he was going to get the guard. We convince him not to and tell him to head back to the river
	- The clergyman tells [[Jorund]] he is needed by the [[Raven Queen]]. He tells the crowd "Their lives are not yours to interpret. " He tells us thst it is unsafe for us to be out and we will be given
	- He is [[Kieran]], the keeper of passage (takes care of funerary  rights). He tells [[Jorund]] that the majority of the city has fallen, the wizards made a tactical decision to pull in the barrier. The population didn't take this well and many are dead. He says the [[Raven Queen]]'s judgement has been halted and the dead begin to curdle.
	- a bell starts to ring, some in the crowd mutter and clasp at the holy symbols they wear, as if in mourning. One man seems to trying to count the number of tolls.
	- Once the tolling ceases [[Kieran]]  continues. We should go to the castle, he leads the way and none follow but all watch us out of sight.
	- Walking through the streets, [[Neverwinter]] is awake despite the apparent late hour. Lanterns glow in doorways, black ribbons attached or being attached to most doors. There is a feeling of mourning which grows the deeper into the city we go. People are bowed in Prayer to a range of gods.
	- The bells toll again, the people seem afraid to look up as the bells toll
	- [[Jorund]] asks what the bells are for, is told the city is in mourning for the presumed death of [[Nand]] and that public formal mourning will begin tomorrow. He was last known to be fighting a powerful star spawn but his body wasn't found. His fight took place yesterday. Some in the city hold the belief that we are harbingers of destruction and evil follows in our wake, that we have made things worse.
	- High above the city but impossibly close is Nihal, looming over the city with a wound leaking green ooze where [[Ultrin]] struck it, as it fights to hold itself together. [[Kieran]] informs us that it appeared after [[Nand]] vanished.
	- [[Lord Neverember]] recovered our gear from the site of the battle.
	- [[Jorund]] tells [[Kieran]] that he met the [[Raven Queen]] and was given the title "watchers blade". [[Kieran]] seems to not entirely believe him and thinks he needs a rest
	- the castle seems badly damaged and crumbling, the western wall apparently hit as if by some huge beast. The courtyard of the castle is filled with chunks of stone that seem to have fallen from its towers
	- [[Kieran]] tells [[Jorund]] to rest and meet him in the atrium at dawn the next day. [[Kieran]] tells him that the city believes that ceremony will save them but the dead are backing up and this must be resolved
	- The gates to the castle open, leading to a quieter space as the sounds of the city are muted by the stones of the castle. The gates close behind us.
	- It is unpleasantly cold within the castle, as the corridors are unreasonably grand and lit by non-magical lanterns. Guards line the passage in highly polished armour but they appear exhausted. In the reception area of the castle is a well dressed gentleman awaiting us.
	- We are greeted in a matter of fact manner and informed that [[Lord Neverember]] is busy dealing with the city and will deal with us tomorrow. Rooms have been prepaid for us with our gear
		- inquiries into the city council continues, searching for more bad apples. The manor of the [[Ivaran Sylhorn|Minister for Health]] was outside the barrier and therefore probably not a risk.
		-  [[Neverwinter Academy]] currently has no headmaster, but there is increased cooperation with the [[House of Knowledge]] and the crown. The academy is outside the dome,  but the barrier is stretched to protect it. Investigation has started into the crimes of [[Nand]]
		- There is no communication with other cities outside the barrier. There is an infestation of star spawn and rot beyond the barrier.
		- The death toll in the city has increased, but within the barrier there are no longer death by infestation
		- The dead outside the barrier are not dying for lack of funeral. Resources are being focused on those within the barrier.
		- Looking up at [[Nihal]] for too long has a maddening effect. The [[Neverwinter Academy]] is looking into the matter.
		- Fortified caffeinated wine is brought over and served in very fancy glassware.
		- We ask about [[Edgar]] and are promised that they will check the census.
		- We are guided towards our quarters, away from the modern shiny areas into older dustier areas. The walls and stone work are worn through use. We are led to a wing with 4 bedrooms and told they were reserved for the most esteemed guests of the royal family in times gone by and they were prepaid for us. With belongings in the footlocker by the bed. If anything is missing we should inform someone.
		- There are guards posted for our protection. We are allowed to wonder at will but wondering tonight is discouraged. There is less control of those outside the walls
		- [[Merric]] asks about where [[Arlon]] is, won't tell us but indicates he is a "free" man.
		- [[Ultrin]] reads the water clock with no difficulty, it is just before midnight. Dawn will be around 6ish
		- [[Merric]] locks the door to the wing and takes the key.
		- [[Aust]] notices that the magic of his staff is weaker. [[Ultrin]] finds his crystal ball and amulet of the plains are missing. He feels something drawing him toward the bookcase in his room.
		- [[Jorund]] finds the orb of the vale now makes him feel nervous and unsettled. He notices that the amulet of the devout seems "polished"
		- [[Merric]] finds his frost brand scimitar no longer sheds light and cannot suppress the ability to suppress light. The figurine of wondrous power seems to now be inert.
		- [[Aust]] goes out to the courtyard of the castle and meditates. He finds the process more chaotic and less clear than usual. He feels the presence of something vast, ancient and unnatural around him, reminding him of the feeling of a vast shared consciousness. Every time the bells ring, the feeling of something pressing against nature sharpens. There is a feeling of countless particulates in the air around him. Every time the bells toll the sound and vibration move these, encouraging nature in a way it doesn't want/need. Nature lacks the power to resist enough, it is losing the fight to maintain the balance
		- The others can see the dust in the room is disturbed by every vibration from the bells.

retrieved.heading: Corruption and Destabilization
retrieved.document:
## Corruption and Destabilization

[[Nihal]]'s solar flare pulses are actively consuming the Weave, threatening to unravel all magical structure and cause the gods themselves to fade from existence. This consumption has rendered the Weave vulnerable to external manipulation and has fundamentally weakened magic throughout reality.

Following [[Nand]]'s ritual sacrifice during the events at [[Neverwinter]], the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon [[Ultrin]], transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

The consequences have been severe: magic throughout the world is now significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to [[Neverwinter Academy]] experiences particularly volatile magical instability, making the Weave unpredictable and dangerous to those who channel it.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The Academy operates largely outside the main magical barrier protecting [[Neverwinter]], though the barrier has been stretched to provide marginal protection. Access is gained via an unstable magical tunnel that distorts the Weave and reality itself—magical runes light up to strengthen the barrier as travelers pass through. The institution contains specialized magical rooms and crop-growing facilities that are permanently integrated into the structure and cannot be relocated without moving the entire building.

The Academy maintains advanced runic ward-work capabilities, crafting protective glyphs for vessels that allow them to float above waves. Scholars within practice sophisticated magical disciplines, and the institution historically grew magical reagents, though these have begun dying in recent years.

- We tasked [[Nand]] with researching [[Machanis]],  the [[The Fraternity of Order]], the [[Fortress of Disciplined Enlightenment]] and [[Layer 222 of the abyss]]#Jorund #Merric #Ultrin #jorund #merric #neverwinter #ravenqueen #session-9 #ultrin #year-2024

## At [[Castle Never]]

- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] approached [[Ultrin]]. This was someone who joined the [[Neverwinter Academy]] around the same time as [[Ultrin]], but followed a more normal path, working more closely with politicians. She is used to hiding her feelings, but [[Ultrin]] can see her studying him
	- "I had started preparing myself to hear your name at the funeral, yet here you stand, returned. I am glad I was wrong. You have made yourself interesting and important."
	- "You will be aware by now that [[Neverwinter Academy]] has no head, and unfortunately for you [[Nand]] saw something in you and entrusted everything to you. The academy is split, but they will all look to you and try to convince you they are right. You may have to decide who is right"
	-  [[Ultrin]] asks who she favours. She says what is being done to the barrier should be impossible, only after [[Nand|Archmage Nandntiersanon]]'s spell did it become possible, and [[Ultrin]] was the material component of the spell. 
	- She asks if [[Ultrin]] knows why the weave has changed. He says that he isn't sure, he hasn't done the research. Claims you can see [[The Weave]]  in the same way you can see light and that [[Nand]] has changed [[The Weave]], in an attempt to block [[Nihal]], although in turn [[Nihal]] used the alignment of the plains to its own advantage. 
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests that [[Ultrin]] could help those that seek to "finish" the work of [[Nand]]. Or join those that wish to abandon the [[Neverwinter Academy]] and allow the barrier to stop manipulating [[The Weave]]. Or join those who feel that [[Neverwinter Academy]] is more important than the rest of the city, as only they can fight cosmic evils.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests there are 100s of wizards still in [[Neverwinter Academy]] at varying levels, of which 3 have formed an acting council:
		- [[Professor History]] believes that we should sacrifice [[Neverwinter Academy]]
		- [[Dean Maverin Soul]], master of adjuration, thinks that [[Neverwinter Academy]] should be teleported somewhere and be floating above us.
		- [[Thameor Vex]] is a ritualist, believes that magic shouldn't be as prescriptive as the books say, that magic can be more powerful if we get rid of the guardrails, and this will unlock power to fight out enemies.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] mentions that magic has shifted and spells and enchantments have changed. Most magic is now less powerful.
	- [[Ultrin]] asks why the magic artefacts aren't just taken out of [[Neverwinter Academy]] and why moving it has taken such a following. Apparently walking through the umbilical cord to the city is quite an experience. 
		- Magic that would have previously been quick, easy and predictable that would be useful for moving things - now no longer are
		- The magical rooms could not be used without moving the whole building. 
		- The space for growing crops would also be lost without taking the whole building
		- [[Ultrin]] is against a magical [[Neverwinter Academy]] hovering over the city and thinks it should just land somewhere. [[Ultrin]] suggests that [[Neverwinter Academy]] could be moved to the river, as there's lots of space there, just build a platform first.
	- [[Ultrin]] suggests that a powerful banishment might be able to send [[Nihal]] away. [[Magister Eloin Vale]] asks why [[Nand]] would bring the creature closer, but [[Ultrin]] says that he doesn't believe that was a part of [[Nand]]'s plan and the creature took advantage of things (unless something went wrong).
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] says she will head to [[Neverwinter Academy]] and share what [[Lord Neverember]] has said today. [[Ultrin]] asks [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to prepare for [[Ultrin]] visiting. She says that she has a small research team that is currently investigating [[The Weave]] but perhaps that is irrelevant and irreversible, so they could change to another topic if [[Ultrin]] prefers. 
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for research into mass banishment & mass dispel curse 
		- [[Ultrin]] briefly has a fleeting thought about asking them to research lich spells, but realises this isnt his thought and turns it away.
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for a 500GP jewel for "spell components"
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] also mentions that [[Neverwinter Academy]] will be interested in the rest of the party as "material components" in a spell. [[Ultrin]] suggests that the party probably isnt aware of this and should just be called material witnesses. 
- At this point the doors of the chamber open and the crowd starts to disperse around the city, in a variety of conversations on the way out.
- [[Ultrin]] says to the party that we need to visit [[Neverwinter Academy]] so he can use his influence to do what's right, but [[Ultrin]] ==doesn't know whats right==. 
	- [[Jorund]] uses message to ask [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to bring some seeds for [[Aust]]. She walks over and asks what sort of plants, is told those for growing healing potion reagents & food. [[Aust]] passes over the list from [[Guildmistress Rayleen]]
- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] each take one of the ==war ponies== that the city guard use. [[Aust]] is mildly unimpressed at such use of an animal when they could just walk.
	- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] head to the [[House of Knowledge]]
	- [[Ultrin]] & [[Aust]] head to [[Neverwinter Academy]]
- [[Jorund]] doesn't notice much on the trip. People around are facedown, not looking at the sky. 
	- [[Merric]] and [[Ultrin]] look up and see the wounded star hanging in the sky. It has a green wound from where it was struck. There is a mist or light bleeding out of it. Around the edges of the wound is rippling, as if burning at the edges. For a moment the glow stops and it looks as if something is shifting within the wound.
	- [[Ultrin]] can see that it looks like a beating heart, with rhythmic pulsing. Thin black threads are emitted from each pulse and descend towards the city, brushing against the barrier and dissipating. Each time the barrier is touched in this way it shimmers, showing some pressure.
	- Every time there is a tolling of a bell the heart pulses.
	- [[Ultrin]] and [[Merric]] are both reminded of what happened to [[The Weave]] And the infinite threads of light being pulled towards the ship, except this is darkness & shadow. It feels as if [[Nihal]] is reaching for something, but the barrier is preventing it, but struggling.
	- The [[Starspawn of Nihal]] swarming the barrier are currently all stationary, which feels wrong.
	- [[Aust]] points out that pack animals will rest when a wounded prey is seriously enough wounded that the battle is won and they can wait for their prey to die.
	- [[Merric]] gets the sense that the mourning bells aligning everyone to think of the same thing has power in it. The tolling is disturbing the star and focusing on the city, but the barrier is also shimmering as if the barrier is being strengthened. It feels as if the bells are helping and hindering.
		- [[Jorund]] knows the tolling is ritualistic, unifying the city and powering the ritual of sending a soul to rest, but that isnt happening here and could potentially be subverted. Also religion isnt working as it should & a city wide ritual is perhaps susceptible to corruption. 
	- After about 10-15 seconds the strands from the star stop. 
	- [[Jorund]] suggests moving up the timetable for the funeral, rather than just stopping the bells which may make the populous afraid. 
- [[Jorund]] talks briefly to a guard and tells him to keep believing. The guard asks what to believe in, and [[Jorund]] tells him to believe in the [[Raven Queen]]. 
	- They chat about how [[Lord Neverember]] will help the party liberate [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]

## Overview

[[Ultrin]] is a mysterious figure with deep connections to arcane magic and divine forces. He possesses knowledge of forbidden rituals and ancient texts, demonstrating sophisticated understanding of The [[Weave]] and its manipulation. His ability to channel divine power through himself and redirect reality-altering spells marks him as an extraordinarily powerful spellcaster or conduit for greater forces.

retrieved.heading: Role in the Campaign

## Role in the Campaign

Ultrin played a crucial role in the climactic events of the [[Neverwinter]] arc. He orchestrated the final ritual against the worm star, persuading the party to participate by framing their involvement as an expression of fate and divine will. His command of forbidden magical knowledge and ability to wield reality-bending forces made him indispensable to confronting the cosmic threat. Whether he acted as a willing agent of higher powers or pursued his own agenda remains unclear.

The book played a critical role in resolving the worm infestation threatening [[Neverwinter]]. Its intervention proved decisive in both combating the immediate threat and affecting celestial bodies, demonstrating power at scales far beyond typical magic. The circumstances surrounding how [[Ultrin]] obtained the book and his connection to it remain unclear, suggesting deeper mysteries yet to be uncovered.

## Capabilities & Properties

The book's most documented power is its ability to channel and manipulate fundamental magical forces. During the crisis in [[Neverwinter]], it enabled [[Ultrin]] to perform a spell against the worms while simultaneously redirecting [[The Weave]]'s power at a star. After this working was completed, the book absorbed the remainder of [[The Weave]] before snapping shut, suggesting it has limitations or safeguards that activate after expending its power.

[[Ultrin]] claims that the book contains a portion of his soul, indicating either a voluntary binding or forced connection between the artifact and its wielder. This claim raises questions about whether the book requires a soul component to function at its full potential, or whether this is specific to [[Ultrin]]'s circumstances.

input.query: Nand ritual weave power Ultrin material component

content.text:
Based on the campaign notes, here's why Ultrin is so important to both Nand and the Neverwinter barrier:

## Ultrin's Critical Importance

**Connection to Nand:**
In Session 34, Nand sacrificed himself during a ritual against the worms at a ship's prow. Before collapsing, Nand transferred incomprehensible weave-power directly to **Ultrin**, making him the material component of the spell. This wasn't incidental—Nand specifically entrusted everything to Ultrin, recognizing something in him that made him essential to the ritual's success.

**Connection to the Neverwinter Barrier:**
The barrier protecting Neverwinter was created by Nand's spell, but its continued function is fundamentally dependent on Ultrin. Here's why:

1. **Ultrin absorbed The Weave itself**: During Nand's ritual, an incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon Ultrin. The threads of The Weave became tangible and condensed permanently into him, granting him a direct connection to magical reality itself.

2. **He's the anchor point**: The barrier's integrity is tied to this connection. Magister Eloin Vale explicitly tells Ultrin in Session 38 that "only after [[Nand|Archmage Nandntiersanon]]'s spell did it become possible" to manipulate the barrier as it currently is, and that **Ultrin was the material component of the spell**—meaning the barrier's very existence is bound to him.

3. **He can perceive and influence The Weave**: Ultrin can see The Weave in the same way others see light, allowing him to understand how Nand changed it and how it's being manipulated. This makes him uniquely positioned to maintain, repair, or modify the barrier.

In essence, Ultrin is the living conduit through which Nand's protective magic flows—without him, the barrier that shields Neverwinter from the Starspawn of Nihal would likely fail.

answer:
Based on the session notes, **Neverwinter Academy has been developing a protective magical device** that could shield the party in Neverdeath Graveyard.

**The danger of using it:** The device risks **weakening Neverwinter's primary magical barrier**. Analysis suggests that implementing this untested magical solution could actually weaken the barrier rather than strengthen it, potentially creating openings large enough to allow the **Starspawn of Nihal to breach the city's defenses**. This is particularly concerning given that the barrier has a history of catastrophic failure, with previous contractions resulting in significant loss of life.

Vale, from the Academy, outlined this dilemma when proposing that academy wizards could craft the device for the party—the work necessary to create it would necessarily weaken existing protective barriers during an active siege situation, forcing difficult decisions about Neverwinter's overall protection strategy.

**The danger of using it:** The device risks **weakening Neverwinter's primary magical barrier**. Analysis suggests that implementing this untested magical solution could actually weaken the barrier rather than strengthen it, potentially creating openings large enough to allow the **Starspawn of Nihal to breach the city's defenses**. This is particularly concerning given that the barrier has a history of catastrophic failure, with previous contractions resulting in significant loss of life.

Vale, from the Academy, outlined this dilemma when proposing that academy wizards could craft the device for the party—the work necessary to create it would necessarily weaken existing protective barriers during an active siege situation, forcing difficult decisions about Neverwinter's overall protection strategy.

answer:
<thinking>I have found information about signs of corruption at Neverdeath Graveyard in the session notes.</thinking>

Signs of corruption at Neverdeath Graveyard include:

1. **Cracked and Decaying Mausoleums**: Multiple mausoleums have developed unexplained cracks and signs of decay that appear unnatural—some occurring overnight without previous damage. These corrupted structures are notably not dedicated to Raven Queen faithful, suggesting deliberate desecration.

2. **Supernatural Disturbances**: The graveyard has experienced several supernatural disturbances, including an underground chamber beneath the Neverwinter Temple of the Raven Queen with a trapdoor showing signs of corrosive damage and claw marks, suggesting something emerged from below.

3. **Ritual Corruption**: There is evidence of corrupted funerary rites being performed at the location.

4. **Star Spawn Behavior**: Star spawn from the sky have been observed hesitating to attack the mausoleums, implying they may contain protective wards or represent areas of significant power.

These signs indicate that Neverdeath Graveyard has become a site of significant supernatural activity and potential corruption.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The graveyard has experienced several supernatural disturbances. During plague investigations, an underground chamber beneath the [[Neverwinter Temple of the Raven Queen]] was discovered with a trapdoor showing signs of corrosive damage and claw marks, suggesting something emerged from below. Later, the graveyard became a site of apparent ritual corruption, with multiple mausoleums cracking and decaying in unnatural ways. Star spawn from the sky have been observed hesitating to attack the mausoleums, implying they may contain protective wards or represent areas of significant power. The party has discovered evidence of corrupted funerary rites being performed at the location.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The graveyard is a substantial cemetery featuring numerous graves, mausoleums, and sepulchers. Several mausoleums have been mapped and recorded by the [[House of Knowledge]]. In recent times, multiple mausoleums have developed unexplained cracks and signs of decay that appear unnatural—some occurring overnight without previous damage. These corrupted structures are notably not dedicated to [[Raven Queen]] faithful, suggesting deliberate desecration. The graveyard also maintains a large ceremonial pyre that guards from multiple factions have staffed to handle plague victims during periods of sickness outbreaks.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party first became aware of Neverember's potential involvement in the plague investigation when his official seals (or convincing forgeries) were discovered on crates at [[My Chemical Reagents]] containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders given to the [[Tarrith]], though the party later learned Tarrith was not directly employed by him and received instructions via dead drops—suggesting his seal may have been forged or misused.

By Session 35-36, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]], privately confessing the city's desperate state while maintaining a facade of authority. He reconciled with [[Arlon]] and revealed that [[Edgar]] died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate. Most significantly, in Session 37, he convened faction representatives at [[Castle Never]] and delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority, pledging to personally accompany them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]].

retrieved.heading: 🎒 Items & Loot
retrieved.document:
## 🎒 Items & Loot

- [[Neverdeath Graveyard blueprints]] — [[House of Knowledge]] — mausoleum records being pulled; cracked mausoleum locations identified on the map
- [[500GP jewel]] — requested by [[Ultrin]] from [[Magister Eloin Vale]] for spell components; not yet received

---

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During the investigation into corrupted funerary practices, Sister Maelin confirmed that the [[Raven Queen]] did not willingly enable or sanction the corrupted rites that had been discovered. She identified approximately 2-3 cracked mausoleums in a rough area of [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] as locations of concern. She also made the practical decision to defer the funeral of [[Nand]] until after the party's planned mission to the graveyard, explaining that tolling funeral bells prevent spirits from dispersing to other planes—a consideration that affects both spiritual protocol and tactical timing.

answer:
<thinking>The session notes provide information about the changes at Neverwinter Academy since Nand's death. I will summarize the key points.</thinking>

Since Nand's death, the Neverwinter Academy has been paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, disagreeing fundamentally on solutions to the institutional crisis: whether to relocate the Academy, preserve forbidden knowledge, or focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting Neverwinter. Information-sharing with the House of Knowledge has decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge. The Academy was identified as a target for destruction by an entity in Merric's dream during plague investigations. It collaborates with local druids on water purification crystal maintenance, funded by the Council of Neverwinter.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Neverwinter Academy is a major magical institution and center of scholarly research in [[Neverwinter]]. It serves as the primary supplier of magical items to the [[Neverwinter Guard]], maintains potion production facilities that supply the city with 2-10 potions nightly on demand, and houses hundreds of wizards at varying skill levels. The Academy coordinates with the city government, the [[House of Knowledge]], and local druids on matters of magical significance and public safety.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The Academy operates largely outside the main magical barrier protecting [[Neverwinter]], though the barrier has been stretched to provide marginal protection. Access is gained via an unstable magical tunnel that distorts the Weave and reality itself—magical runes light up to strengthen the barrier as travelers pass through. The institution contains specialized magical rooms and crop-growing facilities that are permanently integrated into the structure and cannot be relocated without moving the entire building.

The Academy maintains advanced runic ward-work capabilities, crafting protective glyphs for vessels that allow them to float above waves. Scholars within practice sophisticated magical disciplines, and the institution historically grew magical reagents, though these have begun dying in recent years.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Dean Maverin Soul is a master of adjuration magic and serves as one of three acting council members at [[Neverwinter Academy]]. He represents a progressive faction within the Academy's leadership, advocating for innovative magical solutions to the institution's challenges.

retrieved.heading: At the [[Neverwinter Academy]]
retrieved.document:
## At the [[Neverwinter Academy]]

- the path there is unsettling - magic has been stretched to achieve this and is making reality unstable. [[The Weave]] here feels wrong - volatile. There is also a feeling like the tunnel (the "umbilical)" is reacting to their presence, as if there was some gravitational pull and every step causes it to extend and resize. 
	- Magical runes within the barrier light up as they walk past, seeming to make the barrier stronger.
	- [[Aust]] feels a bit weird, but doesn't seem to have the same reaction. He feels like he is walking over roots and plants with every step. [[Ultrin]] swears that he can hear the sound of pages turning with every step.
- Eventually they reach [[Neverwinter Academy]]  and things feel more normal. They can hear the bells tolling and the sound of boots on stone as well as shouting.
	- Gathered in the courtyard is a crowd/mob, arguing with each other and focused on someone at the centre of the crowd. A man in robes appears to be standing on something, wearing dishevelled robes - this is a lecturer called [[Master Alevar]] (a lecturer in the planes and the history of the planes. Found of telling horror stories about the planes to *educate* people).
	- [[Aust]] approaches the crowd. [[Ultrin]] announces his return to the nearest of those in the crowd, they don't care but suggests he stops [[Master Alevar]], who is very riled up
	- [[Master Alevar]] declaims "We gather under these towers as a shelter, but they are built on magic. After the spell plague, schools were built around it. But now the heavens bleed and worms answer spells. Yet people ask the wizards to save you from the horrors that they caused. [[Nand]] is called a hero for bending the world but failing to break it. Perhaps breaking the world would be a mercy. Perhaps cauterising the wound would be best. Would a lich survive the death of magic? Would the [[Starspawn of Nihal]] survive without [[The Weave]] to feed upon? Salvation will come if we stop worshipping the thing that gave birth to this evil."
	- Some seem deeply offended by this, others think he has a point and that wizards *are* the bad guys. A student screams that he's a traitor and the guards move into the crowd (reluctantly), intent on breaking up the situation. They seize [[Master Alevar]], who doesn't fight, but does spot [[Ultrin]], displays no real emotions.
- [[Ultrin]] jumps up onto the vacated platform. He is immediately heckled, both positively and negatively. [[Ultrin]] urges the airing of all views, but in discussion, which [[Ultrin]] promises he will ensure happens. 
	- Some people like this, others hate the idea.

- [ ] Find a way to stop the groups worshipping Nihal
- At HoK
	- [x] Blueprints for the graveyard
	- [ ] tunnels in/to graveyard
	- [ ] How/if disrupt bad rituals
	- [ ] Start the funeral ASAP (are the bells hurting us?)
	- [ ] Stop destroying stuff
	- [ ] Find [[Jarick]]
- At NA
	- [ ] Magic items to help us?
	- [ ] Seeds
	- [ ] Can reshape the shield to cover graveyard? (briefly) & weird portable shield item
	- [ ] Politics

retrieved.heading: Role in the Campaign
retrieved.document:
## Role in the Campaign

Thameor serves as one of three acting council members at [[Neverwinter Academy]], granting him significant authority over institutional policy and magical curricula. In this capacity, he actively works to challenge and dismantle what he views as outdated prescriptive guardrails on magical research and practice. His position allows him to influence both academy operations and broader policy discussions regarding magical regulation within [[Neverwinter]].

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Nand's most critical act was sacrificing himself during a ritual against worms at a ship's prow in Session 34. He activated a spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to [[Ultrin]] before collapsing, fundamentally weakening himself in the process. Shortly thereafter, he disappeared while fighting a powerful star spawn, and his body was never recovered. His death triggered formal mourning throughout [[Neverwinter]], though investigations subsequently revealed he had committed crimes. The barrier currently protecting [[Neverwinter]] was created by Nand's spell, leaving the [[Neverwinter Academy]] dependent on his continued presence and guidance.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Following the death of [[Archmage Nand]], the Academy has been paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, disagreeing fundamentally on solutions to the institutional crisis: whether to relocate the Academy, preserve forbidden knowledge, or focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]]. Information-sharing with the [[House of Knowledge]] has decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge.

The Academy was identified as a target for destruction by an entity in [[Merric]]'s dream during plague investigations. It collaborates with local druids on water purification crystal maintenance, funded by the [[Council of Neverwinter]].

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Nand was a scholar and figure of authority at the [[Neverwinter Academy]], deeply associated with the [[House of Knowledge]]. He possessed extensive knowledge of arcane matters, forbidden artifacts, and planar lore, serving as a key intellectual resource for the city of [[Neverwinter]]. His expertise encompassed dangerous subjects including evil artifacts, forbidden texts, and otherworldly entities. By the campaign's progression, Nand revealed himself to be far more than a mere academic—a powerful practitioner of weave-magic with the ability to manipulate reality-altering rituals.

retrieved.heading: Corruption and Destabilization
retrieved.document:
## Corruption and Destabilization

[[Nihal]]'s solar flare pulses are actively consuming the Weave, threatening to unravel all magical structure and cause the gods themselves to fade from existence. This consumption has rendered the Weave vulnerable to external manipulation and has fundamentally weakened magic throughout reality.

Following [[Nand]]'s ritual sacrifice during the events at [[Neverwinter]], the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon [[Ultrin]], transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

The consequences have been severe: magic throughout the world is now significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to [[Neverwinter Academy]] experiences particularly volatile magical instability, making the Weave unpredictable and dangerous to those who channel it.

answer:
Based on the campaign notes, Neverwinter Academy has undergone significant changes since Nand's death:

## Institutional Crisis and Leadership Vacuum
Following Archmage Nand's sacrifice in Session 34, the Academy has been **paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings**. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, fundamentally disagreeing on the Academy's future:
- Whether to relocate the Academy
- Whether to preserve forbidden knowledge
- Whether to focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting Neverwinter

## Magical Instability
The Academy's connection to the Weave has become dangerously unstable. The tunnel providing access to the Academy now experiences **volatile magical instability**, with reality itself feeling distorted. The barrier protecting the Academy was created by Nand's spell, leaving the institution dependent on his continued presence—a dependency that no longer exists.

## Reduced Information Sharing
Information-sharing with the House of Knowledge has **decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge**, reflecting the Academy's uncertainty and caution in the post-Nand era.

## Political Turmoil
By Session 38 (2026-05-26), the Academy was experiencing open conflict, with figures like Master Alevar publicly questioning whether wizards themselves are responsible for the crises facing Neverwinter, and whether magic itself should be abandoned. This reflects deep institutional doubt and division.

## Practical Constraints
The party discovered that the Academy's magical artifacts and crops cannot be relocated without moving the entire structure intact, complicating any proposed solutions to the Academy's crisis.

	- Some seem deeply offended by this, others think he has a point and that wizards *are* the bad guys. A student screams that he's a traitor and the guards move into the crowd (reluctantly), intent on breaking up the situation. They seize [[Master Alevar]], who doesn't fight, but does spot [[Ultrin]], displays no real emotions.
- [[Ultrin]] jumps up onto the vacated platform. He is immediately heckled, both positively and negatively. [[Ultrin]] urges the airing of all views, but in discussion, which [[Ultrin]] promises he will ensure happens. 
	- Some people like this, others hate the idea.

## Key Events

Following the death of [[Archmage Nand]], the Academy has been paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, disagreeing fundamentally on solutions to the institutional crisis: whether to relocate the Academy, preserve forbidden knowledge, or focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]]. Information-sharing with the [[House of Knowledge]] has decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge.

The Academy was identified as a target for destruction by an entity in [[Merric]]'s dream during plague investigations. It collaborates with local druids on water purification crystal maintenance, funded by the [[Council of Neverwinter]].

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During the party's involvement with [[Neverwinter Academy]], Dean Soul's teleportation proposal became a central point of discussion regarding the Academy's future. The party identified critical complications with his plan: the Academy houses magical artifacts and crops that cannot be relocated without moving the entire structure intact. This discovery highlighted the practical limitations of even sophisticated magical solutions and the interconnected nature of the Academy's magical and material infrastructure.

content.text: I'll search the campaign notes for information about Neverwinter Academy and Nand's death.

Based on the campaign notes, Neverwinter Academy has undergone significant changes since Nand's death:

## Institutional Crisis and Leadership Vacuum
Following Archmage Nand's sacrifice in Session 34, the Academy has been **paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings**. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, fundamentally disagreeing on the Academy's future:
- Whether to relocate the Academy
- Whether to preserve forbidden knowledge
- Whether to focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting Neverwinter

## Magical Instability
The Academy's connection to the Weave has become dangerously unstable. The tunnel providing access to the Academy now experiences **volatile magical instability**, with reality itself feeling distorted. The barrier protecting the Academy was created by Nand's spell, leaving the institution dependent on his continued presence—a dependency that no longer exists.

## Reduced Information Sharing
Information-sharing with the House of Knowledge has **decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge**, reflecting the Academy's uncertainty and caution in the post-Nand era.

## Political Turmoil
By Session 38 (2026-05-26), the Academy was experiencing open conflict, with figures like Master Alevar publicly questioning whether wizards themselves are responsible for the crises facing Neverwinter, and whether magic itself should be abandoned. This reflects deep institutional doubt and division.

## Practical Constraints
The party discovered that the Academy's magical artifacts and crops cannot be relocated without moving the entire structure intact, complicating any proposed solutions to the Academy's crisis.

## Overview

Lord Neverember is the Lord Protector of [[Neverwinter]], serving as the official ruler of the city. He wields considerable political and military authority, commanding access to official seals, city resources, and the loyalty of key military figures like [[General Sabine]]. Despite his position of power, his rule appears marked by pragmatism bordering on moral flexibility—he has been rumored to engage in bribery, election rigging, and other underhanded tactics to maintain his grip on power.

answer:
Based on the campaign notes, here's how Lord Neverember's relationship with the party has evolved:

## Early Campaign: Skepticism and Suspicion
Initially, Lord Neverember appeared **skeptical of the party's capabilities**. The party also grew suspicious of him when they discovered his official seals (or convincing forgeries) on crates at My Chemical Reagents containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders to the Tarrith, though this may have involved forged seals or misuse of his authority.

## Mid-Campaign: Pragmatic Alliance
As the crisis deepened, Neverember's stance shifted. He provided crucial support—recovering the party's gear after battle and agreeing to aid them against threats—though he remained somewhat reluctant with financial compensation and evasive about his involvement in suspicious activities. He functioned as a key political figure whose authority directly impacted the party's mission.

## Recent Sessions (35-37): Complete Trust and Delegation
By **Session 36 (January 20, 2026)**, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis. He privately confessed the city's desperate state to the party while maintaining a public facade of authority. He reconciled with Arlon and revealed that Edgar died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate.

In **Session 37 (March 3, 2026)**, the transformation was complete. Neverember delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech—reading from a letter rather than performing—that publicly invested the party with his **full authority as Lord Protector**. He pledged to personally ride with them to Neverdeath Graveyard. Notably, Arlon observed that "Neverember seems genuinely changed," having abandoned political posturing in favor of direct action and transparency about the city's dire circumstances.

## 🎲 Recap

The party attended a gathering at [[Castle Never]], where [[Lord Neverember]] assembled faction representatives from across the city. Each party member worked the room: [[Ultrin]] reconnected with old acquaintance [[Magister Eloin Vale]], [[Aust]] learned of the alchemists' critical reagent shortage and brokered a deal with [[Guildmistress Rayleen]], [[Jorund]] gathered intelligence from [[Brother Coldis Ren]] and [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] about cracked mausoleums in [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] and the timing of [[Nand]]'s funeral, and [[Merric]] kept watch over [[Arlon]] and flagged suspicious smuggler-held artifacts. The session concluded with [[Lord Neverember]] delivering a raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority as Lord Protector, pledging to ride with them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] personally.

## 👥 NPCs This Session

- [[Lord Neverember]] — Lord Protector of Neverwinter — gave a candid, unpolished speech publicly investing the party with his full authority; pledged to personally ride to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]
- [[Arlon]] — Castle Never security officer — stretched thin against Cult remnants; receptive to using smugglers' artifacts; noted Neverember seems genuinely changed
- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] — delegate of the "acting" arcane council, [[Neverwinter Academy]] — old friend of [[Ultrin]]; outlined academy factions and floated the idea of a protective magical device
- [[Captain Mayer]] — leader of the city guard — detailed the catastrophic cost of fighting [[Starspawn of Nihal]]; quietly resigned to her own death but resolved to follow orders
- [[Brother Coldis Ren]] — cleric of [[Oghma]], [[House of Knowledge]] representative — discussed the redaction effort, the [[Grand Scribe]]'s suspicious behaviour, concerns about [[Ultrin]]'s forbidden magic, and offered graveyard blueprints
- [[Guildmistress Rayleen]] — Alchemists' Guild — warned that magical reagents are critically scarce; agreed to bring seeds to the castle for [[Aust]] to accelerate growth
- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] — chief funerary officer, [[Raven Queen]] clergy — gave [[Jorund]] the location of cracked mausoleums in [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]; discussed deferring [[Nand]]'s funeral; confirmed the [[Raven Queen]] did not willingly enable the corrupted rites
