retrieved.document:
## Heading to the [[Halls of Justice]]

- We decide to recruit help from [[Aarlon]] to get someone to keep an eye on [[My Chemical Reagents]]. To get to the [[Halls of Justice]] we walk past the [[Chasm]].
- As we walk past the [[Chasm]] [[Merric]]'s #spell-scar  starts to whisper again. Even using comprehend languages isn't enough to be able to understand. 
- As we reach the Protector's Enclave we find it shut down for the night. The different groups of fighters are out and about. 
	- On a rooftop opposite the [[Halls of Justice]] we spot a dark elven figure who seems surprised to see us spotting them and takes off.
	- We loop around the block but see no further sign of the figure.
- The [[Lords' Alliance]] figures let us into the [[Halls of Justice]] and we head to the throne room. Here, [[Lord Neverember]], [[General Sabine]] and [[Aarlon]] are present, standing over a map of the city covered in figures.
	- The dynamic with [[Aarlon]] seems odd - like a junior partner
	- [[Lord Neverember]] has a fresh looking gnarly scar down one side of his face.#Aust #Jorund #Merric #aust #jorund #merric #neverwinter #session-14 #year-2024

retrieved.heading: [[Castle Never]]
retrieved.document:
## [[Castle Never]]

- [[Ultrin]] is awoken by the sound of the shutters in his room rattling. It sounds like something outside is trying to get in. Despite the fact the windows aren't on the ground floor.
	- [[Ultrin]] opens the shutters to find nothing, except a warm breeze coming in. The wind pauses for a moment. Beyond the barrier he can see hideous creatures from [[The Farm Realm]]. One of them seems to turn to look at him, and then another and another. After a moment they lose interest and the wind returns to normal. The bells resume tolling.
	- Turning back into the room, [[Ultrin]]'s book likes open beside his bed. He remembers it was closed before he went to bed. Glancing into it, writing is visible on an open page - in his own hand, yet hard to interpret, the scribblings of a madman: "Binding, witness, threshold (circled twice) Azothum residue"
	- [[Ultrin]] remembers that Azothum is believed to be the tangible material of the spirit. Scholars debate if spirits are real, but when a living being is transmuted (which is forbidden), the process would leave behind this residue.
	- [[Ultrin]] has a feeling as if half remembering a dream, but can't place the details.
	- [[Ultrin]] feels unaffected and impartial (he tells himself)
- ==[[Merric]] has a nicer experience.==
	- [[Merric]] dreams of the burial stones around his ancestral tree, it's a familiar dream where he always felt like an outsider. Tonight he feels like he belongs and that the circle is whole, the stones are well maintained and the names caved cleaned. [[Merric]] feels at peace, like this is how things are meant to be.
	- [[Merric]] feels a memory surfacing: [[Jungo]] used to tend to this place. He remembers the patience and care that [[Jungo]] used to give this place: it's not the stories that really matter, it's that they end properly. As long as the body is returned to the tree to rest, that's all that really matters. There is nothing to rush for.
	- [[Merric]] also feels a memory of [[Verna]] sitting with him and [[Jungo]] around the campfires. One night she was staring into the woods and warned us that beyond the clan, there are bad things, things that use you rather than killing you and ensure you never have a clean end. This was a formative moment for [[Jungo]]
	- [[Merric]] sees the stones around the tree fade into roots, the tree seems larger than ever and is whole and reassuring for a moment. Then the bark begins to split and sap runs out - too dark and red. The blood flows down into the roots and soil, pulled in a single direction, behind the tree.
	- [[Merric]] heads around the tree, following the blood. One single grave lies open - it was never used  and is filling with blood. The soil within the grave is shifting - worms writhing and occupying the space that should be filled with his kin. The roots of the tree recoil as if in fear. It's unclear whose grave this is.
	- There is a feeling that there is one final story left to be neatly tidied up to let the clan be at rest. The lack of peace in this one grave is impacting the clan.
- [[Merric]] gets an early breakfast and asks for whatever is simple and at hand. There are a lot of servants present.
	- [[Merric]] asks [[Stephen]] if there are a lot of people due for breakfast - is told that there's only the finest of [[Neverwinter]]. There will be a proclamation from [[Lord Neverember]] about the return of the party.
	- [[Merric]] asks about the welfare of the people and whether everyone has enough to eat etc. Is told that there's lots to go around now that there are fewer mouths to feed.
	- [[Aust]], [[Jorund]] and [[Ultrin]] join for breakfast. The staff treat the group like heroes and saviours of the city (unlike the others we met).
- [[Jorund]] wonders whether we should meet our Drow contact for information about the [[Underdark]].
	- ==[[Aust]] gets a bit racist about whether we can trust them==
	- [[Aust]] points out that there are entrances to the [[Underdark]]
	- [[Merric]] asks if there are any Druids left in the grove, [[Aust]] says they seem to have given up.
	- [[Ultrin]] asks [[Niphen]] when the proclamation will be - we are told it will be a few hours, but that [[Lord Neverember]] is expected before then. [[Merric]] tells [[Niphen]] to inform [[Lord Neverember]] to wait if he arrives early.
- ==Dawn is coming. Reluctantly.==
- From the atrium it's possible to look out over the city waking up and starting another working day.
	- [[Kieran]] is waiting for [[Jorund]] and welcomes him. [[Aust]] starts pacing around and [[Merric]] starts reading.
	- [[Kieran]] informs [[Jorund]] that the clergy is at his service, and that his returning was foretold to them ([[Merric]] laughs at this).
	- [[Kieran]] asks what guidance the [[Raven Queen]] offered [[Jorund]] during his audience. [[Jorund]] asks [[Kieran]] what happened on this side of [[Nand]]'s spell. The sky changed a few times and there was a *great* explosion and the wounded star appeared.
	- [[Jorund]] explains what he remembered of the journey we took through the spell. [[Ultrin]] says we maybe shouldn't be telling him this and then goes on to correct [[Jorund]]'s telling.
	- [[Aust]] asks which of the baddies arrayed against us were causing problems for the gods of death. We think it's [[Kyusse]] - which is also [[Nihal]], so may be affected, but we're unsure
	- [[Jorund]] asked about the fate of the [[High Inquisitor]] and is told he was posted to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]. A priest was sent to scout out the area, reporting that several graves have become cracked and decayed in a way that they weren't yesterday, then was swarmed by monsters from the sky. The scout reported that the star spawn seemed hesitant to attack the mausoleums so he had headed towards one. A theory is that there are other [[Raven Queen]] forces hidden there, but [[Kieran]] is unconvinced.
	- [[Kieran]] explains that the current forces of government view the priesthoods as necessary, but that the government is currently making no decisions and a member of the order was invited to the proclamation - [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] as the highest ranking.
	- There are about 10 members of the [[Raven Queen]] clergy Inside the shield, but they find that on performing the rights, their spirits do not pass over.
	- [[Aust]] asks [[Kieran]] to walk amongst the people and ask for news of anyone that might be part of the [[Kyusse]] cult or any other aberrant ideas.
	- [[Merric]] asks [[Kieran]] about the commoners and is told they are looked after. He is told the government is merely maintaining the status quo and is losing the battle.
	- [[Aust]] asks about any resources, but is told they would be held by the inquisition up at [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]
	- [[Aust]] asks where the star spawn could be coming from. [[Ultrin]] thinks that now that [[Nihal]] is here then its easier to manifest fresh star spawn.
	- [[Merric]] is told that all the mirrors in the castle have been destroyed. [[Kieran]] says he must have heard of the [[Hall of Ashen Mirrors]].
	- [[Kieran]] is concerned that the funerary rites have been corrupted. [[Merric]] feels like it may not be as simple as having to just simply sanctify the vessels created in the ritual and that they may need to be destroyed.
	- [[Aust]] suggests there may be single lynchpin corpses that are holding the door to the realms of the dead open to the forces of [[Kyusse]] and that destroying them may help the gods.
	- [[Jorund]] names [[Kieran]] an acting member of the Inquisition and says he will try to get him a messenger bag if we visit [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]
- We return the reception of [[Castle Never]] and await [[Lord Neverember]]. He arrives with great ceremony. As he enters with much authority and pride and very shiny armour, his guards remain outside and the doors close.
	- As soon as he is out of the public eye the facade slumps and he looks exhausted and defeated. Maintaining the public image is taking its toll.
	- "Welcome home. The city is not as you left it. The situation is rapidly worsening and I don't know what to do." [[Merric]] asks him what he's done so far, "politics, symbolism and some kind of order, but I don't know how to contend with such threats. There is a horrific scar in the sky, it is maddening the people. I know the symbolism doesn't help but it is all we have"
	- [[Ultrin]] asks what the academy is advising, is told that it is paralysed by indecision and endless meetings. There are suggestions of moving [[Neverwinter Academy]] or simply sacrificing it. It is unclear how the barrier was moulded to the shape, but many feel it is unnatural. The academy wishes to help but is yet to make a decision in the absence of [[Nand]] (==the *Nand Gate*==)
	- The [[House of Knowledge]] is sharing what they feel is safe, but this reduces every day. They fear sharing the wrong knowledge despite the strong collaboration that exists between all parties.
	- There has been no word from the Druids.
	- [[Lord Neverember]] has invested all hope into our return as so long as we are here the city it will not fall. We mention that we're about to leave. There is a rumour that there is an entrance to the [[Underdark]] through the rift in the city.
	- [[Aust]] explains that the [[Araumycos]] is corrupted by [[Zugtamoid]] (via a corrupted marriage) - the cause of the rot and fungus spores in the air.
	- [[Aust]] suggests that the populous needs to be rallied around the [[Raven Queen]]. [[Lord Neverember]] suggests that the city is currently clinging to any god they feel can save them, suggests that we can turn [[Nand]] into a martyr and that we can turn them to worshipping the [[Raven Queen]] rather than [[Umberlee]] or other gods.
	- [[Merric]] asks about the investigation of the ex-[[Ivaran Sylhorn|Minister for Health]].
	- [[Merric]] asks about [[Arlon]] and is told that he and [[Lord Neverember]] have somewhat made up. [[Merric]] and [[Aust]] mention that [[Arlon]]'s network should round up all the minister's contacts and question them all, especially as there was one at the mansion that was cleaning up and escaped.
	- [[Lord Neverember]] informs us that [[Edgar]] had died in defence of our estate, defending 2 unmarked graves.
	- [[Jorund]] suggests that [[Lord Neverember]] accompany the party on the trip to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]. [[Merric]] insists on a practise duel to test his skills and is mildly impressed.

retrieved.heading: Relationships
retrieved.document:
## Relationships

Neverember works closely with [[General Sabine]] and [[Arlon]], the latter relationship having been strained but recently reconciled. His mention causes visible nervousness in [[Vespar Bookman]], suggesting complex power dynamics or potential blackmail. He appears skeptical of the party's capabilities initially but has grown to trust them as the crisis deepens. His belief that the [[Lords of Waterdeep]] will save the city suggests either genuine faith in external salvation or political calculation.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party first became aware of Neverember's potential involvement in the plague investigation when his official seals (or convincing forgeries) were discovered on crates at [[My Chemical Reagents]] containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders given to the [[Tarrith]], though the party later learned Tarrith was not directly employed by him and received instructions via dead drops—suggesting his seal may have been forged or misused.

By Session 35-36, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]], privately confessing the city's desperate state while maintaining a facade of authority. He reconciled with [[Arlon]] and revealed that [[Edgar]] died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate. Most significantly, in Session 37, he convened faction representatives at [[Castle Never]] and delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority, pledging to personally accompany them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]].

retrieved.heading: 👥 NPCs This Session
retrieved.document:
## 👥 NPCs This Session

- [[Lord Neverember]] — Lord Protector of Neverwinter — gave a candid, unpolished speech publicly investing the party with his full authority; pledged to personally ride to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]
- [[Arlon]] — Castle Never security officer — stretched thin against Cult remnants; receptive to using smugglers' artifacts; noted Neverember seems genuinely changed
- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] — delegate of the "acting" arcane council, [[Neverwinter Academy]] — old friend of [[Ultrin]]; outlined academy factions and floated the idea of a protective magical device
- [[Captain Mayer]] — leader of the city guard — detailed the catastrophic cost of fighting [[Starspawn of Nihal]]; quietly resigned to her own death but resolved to follow orders
- [[Brother Coldis Ren]] — cleric of [[Oghma]], [[House of Knowledge]] representative — discussed the redaction effort, the [[Grand Scribe]]'s suspicious behaviour, concerns about [[Ultrin]]'s forbidden magic, and offered graveyard blueprints
- [[Guildmistress Rayleen]] — Alchemists' Guild — warned that magical reagents are critically scarce; agreed to bring seeds to the castle for [[Aust]] to accelerate growth
- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] — chief funerary officer, [[Raven Queen]] clergy — gave [[Jorund]] the location of cracked mausoleums in [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]; discussed deferring [[Nand]]'s funeral; confirmed the [[Raven Queen]] did not willingly enable the corrupted rites

retrieved.heading: Relationships
retrieved.document:
## Relationships

[[Vespar Bookman]] maintains a transactional relationship with adventurers willing to trade in forbidden knowledge. His nervousness at mention of [[Lord Neverember]] suggests tension or fear regarding Neverwinter's ruling authority, potentially creating conflicting interests depending on party allegiances.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During the party's visit to [[the Shrouded Ledger]], Vespar appraised [[Ultrin's Book of Amulets of Ultimate Evil]] and valued it at 400 gold pieces, confidently stating it would "fly off the shelf"—indicating strong market demand for such dark texts. His demeanor shifted dramatically when [[Ultrin]] mentioned working for [[Lord Neverember]]. Vespar became visibly nervous, suggesting he operates in violation of Neverember's authority and should potentially be subject to arrest. This reaction implies he may have previous run-ins with the law or runs his operation in defiance of official oversight.

retrieved.heading: 👥 NPCs This Session
retrieved.document:
## 👥 NPCs This Session

- [[Kieran]] — keeper of passage, [[Raven Queen]] clergy — confirmed clergy is at the party's service; relayed the scout's report on [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]; named acting inquisition member by [[Jorund]]
- [[Stephen]] — castle servant — provided [[Merric]] with early breakfast and information about the forthcoming proclamation
- [[Niphen]] — castle servant — confirmed the proclamation was a few hours away; instructed by [[Merric]] to have [[Lord Neverember]] wait if he arrived early
- [[Lord Neverember]] — Lord Protector of Neverwinter — confessed the city's desperate state candidly; revealed [[Edgar]]'s death and [[Arlon]]'s reconciliation; agreed to ride to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]; underwent a practice duel with [[Merric]]

retrieved.heading: Notable NPCs
retrieved.document:
## Notable NPCs

[[Lord Neverember]] uses the Halls as his primary base of operations. [[General Sabine]] has been observed gathering here for strategic planning sessions alongside other military leadership and [[Aarlon]].

retrieved.heading: [[Castle Never]]
retrieved.document:
## [[Castle Never]]

- [[Lord Neverember]] leads us to a grand public room with a not-quite-throne, where the proclamation will be given. [[Arlon]] is here, checking security. He seems older and disappointed in how things have turned out.
	- [[Aust]] sits in the not-throne
	- We ask if [[Arlon]] is aware of any other remnants of the Cult, but he says he's stretched thin and struggling to find the personnel to stop it.
	- We ask [[Arlon]] to perhaps provide a distraction. He asks what's up with the [[Raven Queen]] and if we have news of the rest of the world. We tell him that she is under siege and we don't know much, so we'll act locally.
	- He tells us that the [[House of Knowledge]] has 2 factions - one that wants to destroy the knowledge before its lost/misused and one that wants to make use of all the forbidden knowledge.
- About 20 people arrive, wearing the different symbols of their organisations, including the [[Raven Queen]], the [[Neverwinter Academy]] and the [[House of Knowledge]] (who both look a bit "progressive", ie wanting to share, work together and not be a recluse). No druids. Simple food is brought in.
	- [[Merric]] and [[Ultrin]] go to speak to the wizard. [[Magister Eloin Vale]], delegate of the "acting" arcane council ([[Ultrin]] says this isn't a thing). There are now (at least) 3 people wanting to be headmaster, but now is not the time for such things. There are different sects that want to
		- Move the academy (the whole thing) to somewhere, probably inside the barrier so it can be made smaller
		- Others want to know what [[Nand]] was working on, as his plans are not complete.
		- Some want to fight, others wish to preserve knowledge and sacrifice the city. Others think that [[Ultrin]] can save them.
	- They have been working with the [[House of Knowledge]] to try and see if they can affect [[Nihal]]  but they are restricted from dangerous knowledge
	- It may be possible for one of the wizards to put some spell energy into a device that we could take with us to the graveyard that could protect us, but it could weaken the entire barrier and potentially let in [[Starspawn of Nihal]]
- [[Aust]] mingles with [[Captain Mayer]] - the leader of the city guard.
	- The guard is stationed around the perimeter of the barrier in case it shrinks again. They lost a lot of men last time that happened. However there is much crime happening currently. Also, [[General Sabine]] is still in charge.
	- There has been no word (good or bad) of the druids. So they have no idea what's happened.
	- The [[Starspawn of Nihal]] have a weird trait where it can look like you stab them and yet not hit anything and visa versa. Looking at them for too long can cause madness and they can kill you without touching you. Generally 20-30 guardsmen with swords required to take one down, half of them die in the process. For every one killed, a hundred will replace them. Their numbers only increase.
- [[Jorund]] goes to talk to the [[House of Knowledge]] representative, a cleric of [[Oghma]] [[Brother Coldis Ren]], who seems a bit scruffy.
	- Everyone is busy destroying and redacting knowledge, or sealing it in the vault under the [[House of Knowledge]].
	- [[Jorund]] tells him about the [[Sangrullo]] and the pyramids. The priest is surprised he knows about "the island" but tells him it isn't "that one"
	- The priests are currently working overtime to redact things. The [[Grand Scribe]] would need to be convinced to stop the redaction. [[Jorund]] suggests he's acting a bit weirdly because he's been reading too much of the forbidden texts.
	- [[Jorund]] tells him about the plan to head to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] in hopes of finding survivals and/or corrupt rituals to stop. The cleric says the [[House of Knowledge]] would have all the blueprints of the graveyard if they would be useful, but would need to point to the crypts on a map
	- [[Jorund]] asks how things are with the wizards and mentions [[Ultrin]]. He is told some people within [[Neverwinter Academy]] can sometimes create knowledge. But they are deeply concerned by some of the questions that come from there.
	- The priest asks if [[Jorund]] would trust [[Ultrin]] with the knowledge of [[Nihal]]'s apostasy. [[Jorund]] argues for all the things that [[Ultrin]] has done. He is told that mentioning that [[Ultrin]] took part in forbidden magic is exactly the sort of thing that the [[House of Knowledge]] would not want.
	- [[Jorund]] mentions that [[Lord Neverember]] would be joining for the trip to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]. Is told he is a rallying point for the population and that there isn't really anyone that could replace him and we are the people that the people are to be rallied around.
- We decide that [[Ultrin]] shouldn't go to the [[House of Knowledge]], but that [[Aust]] should go with him to [[Neverwinter Academy]]
- [[Merric]] looks for [[Arlon]] - he is mingling. He notices that [[Lord Neverember]] seems to be preparing to give a speech. [[Merric]] asks [[Arlon]] not to vanish after the speech.
	- [[Merric]] sees that [[Lord Neverember]] seems a little off as he produces a written letter and is reading the words out, rather than reading it out loud. He isn't even raising his voice. It seems more a confession than a speech to rouse the city. It speaks to how recently he prepared what he would say and wanting to ensure the wording is exact
	- "You have all heard the bells. You have all seen the sky. The barrier holds but the city does not heal. These 4 were believed lost, their return having faced the thing that wounds the heavens. One tested my resolve with steel, I found it deeply inspiring. I know our city is in good hands. I will not pretend strength I don't posses. The dead do not pass, the rot is rising, time is running out. By my authority as lord protector I invest my authority in these 4. You will give them what they ask for, you will place no barriers in their path. If we are to endure this darkness we will endure it together. [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] will be purified and our dead liberated. I will personally ride with them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]. No more private wars. No more divided banners. We stand not because we are unbroken but because we refuse to kneel."
	- He affixes his seal to the letter and then places it so all can read it.
	- The [[Raven Queen]] representative looks quietly hopeful.
	- The captain of the guard is looking guarded but [[Merric]] can see that she has already accepted that he is going to die. She looks resolved to follow orders.
- [[Ultrin]] goes off to talk to [[Magister Eloin Vale]]. [[Aust]] can tell that they are friends, [[Merric]] can tell that they are old friends and that she appears to have not forgotten him, but [[Ultrin]] seems changed.
- [[Merric]] asks [[Arlon]] what he made of the speech. He liked that [[Lord Neverember]] isn't a pompous prat now and that the time for politics is truly over.
	- [[Merric]] asks [[Arlon]] about crime and the underground. He makes the case for getting the smugglers to hand over/sell/use some of the artifacts that they have acquired.
- [[Aust]] chats with [[Guildmistress Rayleen]] of the alchemists guild. She tells him that magical reagents are becoming scarce, so it's getting harder to brew potions. There are many people that depend on their potions. [[Aust]] asks what she needs and is told that  [[Neverwinter Academy]] has been growing reagents, but these are now dying. She needs primarily anything that could be turned into healing potions and without them the death roll rises.
	- [[Aust]] tells her there is a garden upstairs that seems untainted and could be viable. She says that reagents take time to grow and is unsure if there is time. [[Aust]] responds that perhaps he can aid in accelerating the process. She is surprised there is a living Druid in the city.
	- She agrees to bring seeds to the castle and [[Aust]] promises to do what he can about the tainted garden at [[Neverwinter Academy]].
- [[Jorund]] goes to talk to [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] (chief funerary officer). [[Jorund]] asks how things have been
	- [[Jorund]] explains to her that all the (good) gods of death are under attack. In the absence of other deities the [[Raven Queen]] will look to shepard those souls, but she is currently battling her own issues.
	- [[Jorund]] explains the aims of going to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] to try and find inquisition allies and/or funerary rituals. He explains what we have seen of the corrupted rites through the visions that we saw with [[Nand]] and how it is used to attack the passage of the soul. He is told that shouldn't be possible unless the god is willing. He confirms that when he saw her in person she was not willing.
	- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] states that surely a non-believer could not perform the rites, only someone that the [[Raven Queen]] believed was well intentioned could do this. [[Jorund]] asks if she knew the [[High Inquisitor]] out at [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] - she says vaguely but he was much higher up than her. Perhaps it is possible for the gods to err, being distracted by other matters they could open the passage not being aware of the attack.
	- [[Jorund]] asks what is currently known about [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] and is told that the observer had communicated that certain mausoleums were cracked in ways that they hadn't been previously. She indicates roughly where within [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] they would be (likely 2-3 covered by the area indicated). She mentions that they were not for those of the [[Raven Queen]] faith.
	- [[Jorund]] informs her that it is required to keep the populace faithful and to investigate how it could be possible to corrupt the rites in such a manner. [[Jorund]] suggests connecting with the [[House of Knowledge]] and that he can make an introduction to aid this.
	- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] asks if he met the [[Raven Queen]] if he was dead and she sent him back to them. It is not unknown for her to reject a spirit if it was not their time.
	- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] mentions that the [[Raven Queen]] will be leading the service for [[Nand]] and that it is undecided who should perform it. [[Jorund]] knows the rites having performed them several times. The timing of the funeral is undecided. [[Jorund]] asks if she thinks the mourning is useful. She replies that every time the bells toll the spirits surge as they are called to their place of rest, but there is no such place.
	- [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] is concerned that if the bells stop tolling, the spirits will be drawn elsewhere. They agree that they will wait to see what can be achieved in [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] before they set a time for the funeral.

retrieved.heading: 🎲 Recap
retrieved.document:
## 🎲 Recap

The party attended a gathering at [[Castle Never]], where [[Lord Neverember]] assembled faction representatives from across the city. Each party member worked the room: [[Ultrin]] reconnected with old acquaintance [[Magister Eloin Vale]], [[Aust]] learned of the alchemists' critical reagent shortage and brokered a deal with [[Guildmistress Rayleen]], [[Jorund]] gathered intelligence from [[Brother Coldis Ren]] and [[Sister Maelin Thorn]] about cracked mausoleums in [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] and the timing of [[Nand]]'s funeral, and [[Merric]] kept watch over [[Arlon]] and flagged suspicious smuggler-held artifacts. The session concluded with [[Lord Neverember]] delivering a raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority as Lord Protector, pledging to ride with them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] personally.

retrieved.heading: Role in the Campaign
retrieved.document:
## Role in the Campaign

Neverember functions as a key political figure whose decisions and authority directly impact the party's mission in [[Neverwinter]]. He serves as both potential ally and obstacle: while he has provided crucial support—recovering the party's gear after battle and agreeing to aid them against threats—he has also proven reluctant with financial compensation and sometimes evasive about his involvement in suspicious activities. His willingness to delegate authority to the party while facing the city's crisis suggests he recognizes the limitations of conventional governance in extraordinary circumstances.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Lord Neverember is the Lord Protector of [[Neverwinter]], serving as the official ruler of the city. He wields considerable political and military authority, commanding access to official seals, city resources, and the loyalty of key military figures like [[General Sabine]]. Despite his position of power, his rule appears marked by pragmatism bordering on moral flexibility—he has been rumored to engage in bribery, election rigging, and other underhanded tactics to maintain his grip on power.

His belief that the [[Lords of Waterdeep]] will save the city suggests either genuine faith in external salvation or political calculation.

His geas ties him directly to [[Neverwinter]]'s interests, making him simultaneously constrained by and invested in the city's wellbeing.

His seal also appeared on orders given to the [[Tarrith]], though the party later learned Tarrith was not directly employed by him and received instructions via dead drops—suggesting his seal may have been forged or misused.

By Session 35-36, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]], privately confessing the city's desperate state while maintaining a facade of authority. He reconciled with [[Arlon]] and revealed that [[Edgar]] died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate. Most significantly, in Session 37, he convened faction representatives at [[Castle Never]] and delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority, pledging to personally accompany them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]].

input.query: Neverember first meeting early sessions skeptical

content.text:
Based on the campaign notes, here's how Lord Neverember's relationship with the party has evolved:

## Early Campaign: Skepticism and Suspicion
Initially, Lord Neverember appeared **skeptical of the party's capabilities**. The party also grew suspicious of him when they discovered his official seals (or convincing forgeries) on crates at My Chemical Reagents containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders to the Tarrith, though this may have involved forged seals or misuse of his authority.

## Mid-Campaign: Pragmatic Alliance
As the crisis deepened, Neverember's stance shifted. He provided crucial support—recovering the party's gear after battle and agreeing to aid them against threats—though he remained somewhat reluctant with financial compensation and evasive about his involvement in suspicious activities. He functioned as a key political figure whose authority directly impacted the party's mission.

## Recent Sessions (35-37): Complete Trust and Delegation
By **Session 36 (January 20, 2026)**, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis. He privately confessed the city's desperate state to the party while maintaining a public facade of authority. He reconciled with Arlon and revealed that Edgar died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate.

In **Session 37 (March 3, 2026)**, the transformation was complete. Neverember delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech—reading from a letter rather than performing—that publicly invested the party with his **full authority as Lord Protector**. He pledged to personally ride with them to Neverdeath Graveyard. Notably, Arlon observed that "Neverember seems genuinely changed," having abandoned political posturing in favor of direct action and transparency about the city's dire circumstances.

## Corruption and Destabilization

[[Nihal]]'s solar flare pulses are actively consuming the Weave, threatening to unravel all magical structure and cause the gods themselves to fade from existence. This consumption has rendered the Weave vulnerable to external manipulation and has fundamentally weakened magic throughout reality.

Following [[Nand]]'s ritual sacrifice during the events at [[Neverwinter]], the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon [[Ultrin]], transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

The consequences have been severe: magic throughout the world is now significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to [[Neverwinter Academy]] experiences particularly volatile magical instability, making the Weave unpredictable and dangerous to those who channel it.

retrieved.document:
## Overview

Nand was a scholar and figure of authority at the [[Neverwinter Academy]], deeply associated with the [[House of Knowledge]]. He possessed extensive knowledge of arcane matters, forbidden artifacts, and planar lore, serving as a key intellectual resource for the city of [[Neverwinter]]. His expertise encompassed dangerous subjects including evil artifacts, forbidden texts, and otherworldly entities. By the campaign's progression, Nand revealed himself to be far more than a mere academic—a powerful practitioner of weave-magic with the ability to manipulate reality-altering rituals.

retrieved.heading: 🎲 Recap
retrieved.document:
## 🎲 Recap

Ultrin convinced each party member to join the final ritual against the worms, claiming they were instruments of fate guided by gods. With everyone assembled, Nand sacrificed himself to activate the spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to Ultrin. As the ritual reached its climax, a massive worm eruption tore through the ship's deck, spawning undead creatures and a pulsating heart. The party fought desperately while Ultrin struggled to control the spell, ultimately consulting the forbidden book to redirect the power at the worm star. The spell succeeded catastrophically—the star retreated and vanished, but the ship plummeted from the Far Realm back toward a drastically changed Neverwinter, crashing into the corrupted river.

answer:
## What Happened to Nand and the Consequences

**What Happened:**

In **Session 34** (2025-12-16), Nand sacrificed himself during a critical ritual against the worms at a ship's prow. He activated a spell circle and transferred incomprehensible weave-power to Ultrin before collapsing and becoming fundamentally weakened. Shortly after, he disappeared while fighting a powerful star spawn, and his body was never recovered. His death triggered formal mourning throughout Neverwinter, though subsequent investigations revealed he had committed crimes.

**Consequences:**

1. **Magical Catastrophe**: The Weave was catastrophically altered by Nand's ritual sacrifice. Magic throughout the world became significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to Neverwinter Academy now experiences particularly volatile magical instability.

2. **Loss of Protective Barrier**: The barrier currently protecting Neverwinter was created by Nand's spell, leaving the Neverwinter Academy dependent on his continued presence and guidance—a dependency that can no longer be fulfilled.

3. **Institutional Crisis**: Following Nand's death, the Neverwinter Academy has been paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, disagreeing on whether to relocate the Academy, preserve forbidden knowledge, or focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting Neverwinter. Information-sharing with the House of Knowledge has decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge.

4. **Power Transfer**: Ultrin received the transferred weave-power, granting him infinite magical power temporarily, though the broader magical weakening affected the world at large.

## Role in the Campaign

Nand functioned as both mentor and mysterious manipulator throughout the campaign. He provided crucial information to the party about threats like the [[Sangrullo]], directing them toward dangerous research objectives. He instructed [[Ultrin]] to study the *Talismans of Ultimate Evil* to understand how to handle corrupted artifacts, leveraging payment from the party members for his guidance and knowledge. More ominously, Nand appeared to orchestrate events behind the scenes, arranging rituals and encouraging the party down specific paths. His true agenda—whether benevolent or self-serving—remains unclear.

Despite the grim calculus, the Starspawn have surrounded Neverwinter, assuming a motionless posture around the city's barrier, indicating they have fatally wounded their prey and are simply awaiting its inevitable collapse. The Starspawn threat escalated from the party's first encounter to the current siege in the following ways:

1. **Multiplication and Endless Reinforcements**: Each Starspawn killed is replaced by approximately one hundred more, suggesting a bottomless reserve of reinforcements. This exponential growth in numbers has made the threat overwhelming.

2. **Tactical Patience**: The Starspawn have surrounded Neverwinter and assumed a motionless posture around the city's barrier, indicating they have fatally wounded their prey and are simply awaiting its inevitable collapse. This shows their strategic patience and coordinated intent.

3. **High Casualty Rates**: Each Starspawn requires 20-30 guardsmen to eliminate, with casualty rates averaging 50%. This high attrition rate has severely impacted the city's defenses.

4. **Coordination and Intelligence**: The Starspawn's coordinated attacks and continuous reinforcement suggest organized intent rather than mindless hunger, indicating a high level of intelligence and strategic planning.

5. **Psychic Lethality and Madness**: Prolonged visual contact with the Starspawn induces madness in observers, making conventional warfare extraordinarily costly. Their ability to kill without physical contact suggests some form of reality-warping or psychic lethality.

## Overview

Starspawn are powerful aberrations of cosmic origin, beings of otherworldly nature that defy conventional understanding. These creatures possess an intelligence and malevolence that marks them as significant threats to any adventuring party. The specimen encountered during the [[Plague Investigation]] demonstrated remarkable tactical awareness and adaptability, suggesting these entities are not mere monsters but cunning adversaries capable of strategic combat.

retrieved.heading: Encounters
retrieved.document:
## Encounters

The Starspawn encountered during the party's [[Plague Investigation]] proved to be a multi-round combat threat requiring sustained effort and coordination to overcome. Its ability to teleport freely made it difficult to pin down, while its spellcasting forced the party to remain tactically vigilant. The creature's vulnerability to divine channeling proved instrumental in the party's eventual victory.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

The Starspawn of [[Nihal]] are alien humanoid entities of extraplanar origin that have become an existential threat to [[Neverwinter]]. First encountered during the [[Plague Investigation]] arc when they attacked the [[Druid Grove]], these creatures possess a fundamentally otherworldly nature that defies conventional understanding. Military forces refer to them colloquially as "Sky Spawn." They are characterized by excessive appendages and disturbing kaleidoscopic eyes that seem to operate on incomprehensible wavelengths. The creatures appear to serve some distant, unknowable master or purpose—their coordinated attacks and continuous reinforcement suggest organized intent rather than mindless hunger.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

Starspawn display a formidable array of magical and supernatural capabilities. They can shift between different forms, allowing them to alter their physical appearance and potentially adapt to combat situations. Teleportation comes naturally to them, enabling rapid repositioning across the battlefield to outmaneuver opponents and maintain tactical advantage.

These creatures are proficient spellcasters, capable of casting complex magical effects that can be countered through standard counterspelling. Their multiple eyes suggest enhanced sensory perception, likely granting them superior awareness of their surroundings and opponents' positions.

Most notably, Starspawn demonstrate genuine fear and vulnerability when exposed to divine magic—specifically when channeling divine power—suggesting their cosmic nature has fundamental weaknesses rooted in holy forces. This represents a critical vulnerability despite their otherwise formidable abilities.

The specimen displayed strategic thinking during combat, deliberately moving between party members rather than committing to a single target, suggesting intelligence and the ability to analyze threats dynamically.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

Starspawn possess capabilities that transcend physical vulnerability. While blunt force trauma and magical attacks can kill individual specimens, they demonstrate eldritch properties that allow them to appear solid while remaining intangible—creating an unsettling discrepancy between sight and touch. They can kill without physical contact, suggesting some form of reality-warping or psychic lethality. Prolonged visual contact with these entities induces madness in observers, making conventional warfare extraordinarily costly. Their bodies decay rapidly over time when slain.

The creatures exhibit pack predator intelligence and patience. When surrounding [[Neverwinter]], they have assumed a completely motionless posture around the city's barrier—a behavioral pattern indicating they have fatally wounded their prey and are simply awaiting its inevitable collapse. Their numbers are seemingly infinite; for each specimen killed, approximately one hundred more manifest to replace it, suggesting a bottomless reserve of reinforcements.

answer:
Based on the campaign notes, here's how the Starspawn threat escalated:

## Initial Encounter
The party first encountered Starspawn during the **Plague Investigation arc** when they attacked the **Druid Grove**. This initial specimen was a manageable single-creature threat—a multi-round combat encounter that required coordination and tactical awareness. The creature's teleportation and spellcasting abilities made it challenging, but the party discovered its vulnerability to divine magic, which proved instrumental in their victory.

## Escalation to Full Threat
The threat escalated dramatically as more Starspawn incursions occurred in Neverwinter. What became clear was:

1. **Scale of Lethality**: Military engagements revealed that each Starspawn requires 20-30 guardsmen to eliminate, with casualty rates averaging 50%—far beyond what individual adventurers could handle.

2. **Endless Reinforcements**: The creatures possess a seemingly infinite supply of reinforcements. For each specimen killed, approximately 100 more manifest to replace it, making attrition warfare mathematically unwinnable.

3. **Current Siege Status**: By Session 37, the Starspawn have completely surrounded Neverwinter's barrier. They maintain a motionless posture around the city, exhibiting pack predator intelligence and patience—their behavior suggests they view the city as fatally wounded prey, simply awaiting its inevitable collapse.

The escalation transformed the threat from a dangerous but defeatable encounter into an **existential crisis** for Neverwinter, with the city now under siege by an apparently bottomless cosmic force.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

Starspawn display a formidable array of magical and supernatural capabilities. They can shift between different forms, allowing them to alter their physical appearance and potentially adapt to combat situations. Teleportation comes naturally to them, enabling rapid repositioning across the battlefield to outmaneuver opponents and maintain tactical advantage.

These creatures are proficient spellcasters, capable of casting complex magical effects that can be countered through standard counterspelling. Their multiple eyes suggest enhanced sensory perception, likely granting them superior awareness of their surroundings and opponents' positions.

Most notably, Starspawn demonstrate genuine fear and vulnerability when exposed to divine magic—specifically when channeling divine power—suggesting their cosmic nature has fundamental weaknesses rooted in holy forces. This represents a critical vulnerability despite their otherwise formidable abilities.

The specimen displayed strategic thinking during combat, deliberately moving between party members rather than committing to a single target, suggesting intelligence and the ability to analyze threats dynamically.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

Starspawn possess capabilities that transcend physical vulnerability. While blunt force trauma and magical attacks can kill individual specimens, they demonstrate eldritch properties that allow them to appear solid while remaining intangible—creating an unsettling discrepancy between sight and touch. They can kill without physical contact, suggesting some form of reality-warping or psychic lethality. Prolonged visual contact with these entities induces madness in observers, making conventional warfare extraordinarily costly. Their bodies decay rapidly over time when slain.

The creatures exhibit pack predator intelligence and patience. When surrounding [[Neverwinter]], they have assumed a completely motionless posture around the city's barrier—a behavioral pattern indicating they have fatally wounded their prey and are simply awaiting its inevitable collapse. Their numbers are seemingly infinite; for each specimen killed, approximately one hundred more manifest to replace it, suggesting a bottomless reserve of reinforcements.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During Session 37, Captain Mayer detailed the devastating reality of fighting the [[Starspawn of Nihal]]. She reported that each creature requires 20-30 guardsmen to eliminate, with casualty rates averaging 50%. Most critically, she emphasized that the Starspawn multiply endlessly, making attrition warfare mathematically unwinnable. Despite this grim calculus, she remains committed to following [[Lord Neverember]]'s orders.

retrieved.heading: Encounters
retrieved.document:
## Encounters

Initial encounters during the [[Plague Investigation]] proved manageable, with individual specimens being contained. Later incursions in [[Neverwinter]] revealed their true scale and lethality. Military engagements require 20-30 guardsmen to kill a single creature, with casualty rates approaching 50%. The death of Neverwinter's old Minister of Defence during combat with these entities highlighted their lethal capacity. Current siege conditions show the Starspawn maintaining patient vigil around the city's barrier, their stillness suggesting absolute confidence in ultimate victory through attrition and the city's eventual collapse.

retrieved.heading: Encounters
retrieved.document:
## Encounters

The Starspawn encountered during the party's [[Plague Investigation]] proved to be a multi-round combat threat requiring sustained effort and coordination to overcome. Its ability to teleport freely made it difficult to pin down, while its spellcasting forced the party to remain tactically vigilant. The creature's vulnerability to divine channeling proved instrumental in the party's eventual victory.

content.text: I'll search the campaign notes for information about the Starspawn threat and how it escalated.

Based on the campaign notes, here's how the Starspawn threat escalated:

## Initial Encounter
The party first encountered Starspawn during the **Plague Investigation arc** when they attacked the **Druid Grove**. This initial specimen was a manageable single-creature threat—a multi-round combat encounter that required coordination and tactical awareness. The creature's teleportation and spellcasting abilities made it challenging, but the party discovered its vulnerability to divine magic, which proved instrumental in their victory.

## Escalation to Full Threat
The threat escalated dramatically as more Starspawn incursions occurred in Neverwinter. What became clear was:

1. **Scale of Lethality**: Military engagements revealed that each Starspawn requires 20-30 guardsmen to eliminate, with casualty rates averaging 50%—far beyond what individual adventurers could handle.

2. **Endless Reinforcements**: The creatures possess a seemingly infinite supply of reinforcements. For each specimen killed, approximately 100 more manifest to replace it, making attrition warfare mathematically unwinnable.

3. **Current Siege Status**: By Session 37, the Starspawn have completely surrounded Neverwinter's barrier. They maintain a motionless posture around the city, exhibiting pack predator intelligence and patience—their behavior suggests they view the city as fatally wounded prey, simply awaiting its inevitable collapse.

The escalation transformed the threat from a dangerous but defeatable encounter into an **existential crisis** for Neverwinter, with the city now under siege by an apparently bottomless cosmic force.

<thinking>The session notes provide information about when Ultrin wounded Nihal, but they do not specify the exact date. However, they do mention that it happened during Jorund's soul trial. The cost to Ultrin is not explicitly stated, but it is implied that it had significant consequences.</thinking>

Ultrin wounded Nihal during Jorund's soul trial. The exact date is not specified, but it happened before Session 38 on 2026-05-26. The cost to Ultrin is not explicitly stated in the notes, but it is implied that it had significant consequences.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During [[Jorund]]'s soul trial, Nihal manifested as a consuming void encroaching upon the [[Raven Queen]]'s fortress itself, directly attacking the mechanisms of death and afterlife. She later appeared as a wounded star hanging impossibly over [[Neverwinter]], large enough to darken the sky despite being above the city. [[Ultrin]] struck her, creating the green wound that now marks her presence. The [[Neverwinter Academy]] has begun investigating the maddening effects of observing her directly. She now orchestrates attacks against multiple death gods and maintains the Starspawn siege around [[Neverwinter]], with her heartbeat-like pulses synchronized to the mourning bells tolling throughout the city, suggesting an ominous connection to death itself.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Nihal is an elder evil of catastrophic cosmic proportions—a reborn star spawn that exists partially in the Farm Realm and partially manifested over [[Neverwinter]]. She appears as a vast abyss of worms and rot, covered in rivers of writhing parasites with an intellect and hunger that transcends mortal comprehension. Her presence alone poses an existential threat to multiple planes of existence. The entity bears a grievous wound inflicted by [[Ultrin]], leaving an oozing green lesion that pulses rhythmically like a dying heart, yet this damage has not diminished her power—only made her more desperate and dangerous. Prolonged observation of Nihal induces madness in those who gaze upon her, and even her appearance defies natural law, manifesting as impossibly large despite hovering above the city.

retrieved.heading: Connections
retrieved.document:
## Connections

Nihal commands or attracts vast numbers of [[Starspawn]] who surround her in motionless vigil, acting as extensions of her will or hunger. The [[Raven Queen]] is among her direct targets, suggesting conflict over dominion of death and the afterlife. The [[House of Knowledge]] restricts information about Nihal's nature and origins, classifying her as too dangerous for unprepared practitioners to learn about. [[Ultrin]] has directly engaged her, inflicting the wound that now marks her sky-presence, though the cost and consequences of this action remain unclear. [[Neverwinter]] itself has become her focal point, with the city's barrier serving as the last defense against her encroachment into the Prime Material Plane.

retrieved.heading: At the [[Neverwinter Academy]]
retrieved.document:
## At the [[Neverwinter Academy]]

- the path there is unsettling - magic has been stretched to achieve this and is making reality unstable. [[The Weave]] here feels wrong - volatile. There is also a feeling like the tunnel (the "umbilical)" is reacting to their presence, as if there was some gravitational pull and every step causes it to extend and resize. 
	- Magical runes within the barrier light up as they walk past, seeming to make the barrier stronger.
	- [[Aust]] feels a bit weird, but doesn't seem to have the same reaction. He feels like he is walking over roots and plants with every step. [[Ultrin]] swears that he can hear the sound of pages turning with every step.
- Eventually they reach [[Neverwinter Academy]]  and things feel more normal. They can hear the bells tolling and the sound of boots on stone as well as shouting.
	- Gathered in the courtyard is a crowd/mob, arguing with each other and focused on someone at the centre of the crowd. A man in robes appears to be standing on something, wearing dishevelled robes - this is a lecturer called [[Master Alevar]] (a lecturer in the planes and the history of the planes. Found of telling horror stories about the planes to *educate* people).
	- [[Aust]] approaches the crowd. [[Ultrin]] announces his return to the nearest of those in the crowd, they don't care but suggests he stops [[Master Alevar]], who is very riled up
	- [[Master Alevar]] declaims "We gather under these towers as a shelter, but they are built on magic. After the spell plague, schools were built around it. But now the heavens bleed and worms answer spells. Yet people ask the wizards to save you from the horrors that they caused. [[Nand]] is called a hero for bending the world but failing to break it. Perhaps breaking the world would be a mercy. Perhaps cauterising the wound would be best. Would a lich survive the death of magic? Would the [[Starspawn of Nihal]] survive without [[The Weave]] to feed upon? Salvation will come if we stop worshipping the thing that gave birth to this evil."
	- Some seem deeply offended by this, others think he has a point and that wizards *are* the bad guys. A student screams that he's a traitor and the guards move into the crowd (reluctantly), intent on breaking up the situation. They seize [[Master Alevar]], who doesn't fight, but does spot [[Ultrin]], displays no real emotions.
- [[Ultrin]] jumps up onto the vacated platform. He is immediately heckled, both positively and negatively. [[Ultrin]] urges the airing of all views, but in discussion, which [[Ultrin]] promises he will ensure happens. 
	- Some people like this, others hate the idea.

- [ ] Find a way to stop the groups worshipping Nihal
- At HoK
	- [x] Blueprints for the graveyard
	- [ ] tunnels in/to graveyard
	- [ ] How/if disrupt bad rituals
	- [ ] Start the funeral ASAP (are the bells hurting us?)
	- [ ] Stop destroying stuff
	- [ ] Find [[Jarick]]
- At NA
	- [ ] Magic items to help us?
	- [ ] Seeds
	- [ ] Can reshape the shield to cover graveyard? (briefly) & weird portable shield item
	- [ ] Politics

retrieved.heading: At [[Castle Never]]
retrieved.document:
## At [[Castle Never]]

- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] approached [[Ultrin]]. This was someone who joined the [[Neverwinter Academy]] around the same time as [[Ultrin]], but followed a more normal path, working more closely with politicians. She is used to hiding her feelings, but [[Ultrin]] can see her studying him
	- "I had started preparing myself to hear your name at the funeral, yet here you stand, returned. I am glad I was wrong. You have made yourself interesting and important."
	- "You will be aware by now that [[Neverwinter Academy]] has no head, and unfortunately for you [[Nand]] saw something in you and entrusted everything to you. The academy is split, but they will all look to you and try to convince you they are right. You may have to decide who is right"
	-  [[Ultrin]] asks who she favours. She says what is being done to the barrier should be impossible, only after [[Nand|Archmage Nandntiersanon]]'s spell did it become possible, and [[Ultrin]] was the material component of the spell. 
	- She asks if [[Ultrin]] knows why the weave has changed. He says that he isn't sure, he hasn't done the research. Claims you can see [[The Weave]]  in the same way you can see light and that [[Nand]] has changed [[The Weave]], in an attempt to block [[Nihal]], although in turn [[Nihal]] used the alignment of the plains to its own advantage. 
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests that [[Ultrin]] could help those that seek to "finish" the work of [[Nand]]. Or join those that wish to abandon the [[Neverwinter Academy]] and allow the barrier to stop manipulating [[The Weave]]. Or join those who feel that [[Neverwinter Academy]] is more important than the rest of the city, as only they can fight cosmic evils.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests there are 100s of wizards still in [[Neverwinter Academy]] at varying levels, of which 3 have formed an acting council:
		- [[Professor History]] believes that we should sacrifice [[Neverwinter Academy]]
		- [[Dean Maverin Soul]], master of adjuration, thinks that [[Neverwinter Academy]] should be teleported somewhere and be floating above us.
		- [[Thameor Vex]] is a ritualist, believes that magic shouldn't be as prescriptive as the books say, that magic can be more powerful if we get rid of the guardrails, and this will unlock power to fight out enemies.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] mentions that magic has shifted and spells and enchantments have changed. Most magic is now less powerful.
	- [[Ultrin]] asks why the magic artefacts aren't just taken out of [[Neverwinter Academy]] and why moving it has taken such a following. Apparently walking through the umbilical cord to the city is quite an experience. 
		- Magic that would have previously been quick, easy and predictable that would be useful for moving things - now no longer are
		- The magical rooms could not be used without moving the whole building. 
		- The space for growing crops would also be lost without taking the whole building
		- [[Ultrin]] is against a magical [[Neverwinter Academy]] hovering over the city and thinks it should just land somewhere. [[Ultrin]] suggests that [[Neverwinter Academy]] could be moved to the river, as there's lots of space there, just build a platform first.
	- [[Ultrin]] suggests that a powerful banishment might be able to send [[Nihal]] away. [[Magister Eloin Vale]] asks why [[Nand]] would bring the creature closer, but [[Ultrin]] says that he doesn't believe that was a part of [[Nand]]'s plan and the creature took advantage of things (unless something went wrong).
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] says she will head to [[Neverwinter Academy]] and share what [[Lord Neverember]] has said today. [[Ultrin]] asks [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to prepare for [[Ultrin]] visiting. She says that she has a small research team that is currently investigating [[The Weave]] but perhaps that is irrelevant and irreversible, so they could change to another topic if [[Ultrin]] prefers. 
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for research into mass banishment & mass dispel curse 
		- [[Ultrin]] briefly has a fleeting thought about asking them to research lich spells, but realises this isnt his thought and turns it away.
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for a 500GP jewel for "spell components"
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] also mentions that [[Neverwinter Academy]] will be interested in the rest of the party as "material components" in a spell. [[Ultrin]] suggests that the party probably isnt aware of this and should just be called material witnesses. 
- At this point the doors of the chamber open and the crowd starts to disperse around the city, in a variety of conversations on the way out.
- [[Ultrin]] says to the party that we need to visit [[Neverwinter Academy]] so he can use his influence to do what's right, but [[Ultrin]] ==doesn't know whats right==. 
	- [[Jorund]] uses message to ask [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to bring some seeds for [[Aust]]. She walks over and asks what sort of plants, is told those for growing healing potion reagents & food. [[Aust]] passes over the list from [[Guildmistress Rayleen]]
- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] each take one of the ==war ponies== that the city guard use. [[Aust]] is mildly unimpressed at such use of an animal when they could just walk.
	- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] head to the [[House of Knowledge]]
	- [[Ultrin]] & [[Aust]] head to [[Neverwinter Academy]]
- [[Jorund]] doesn't notice much on the trip. People around are facedown, not looking at the sky. 
	- [[Merric]] and [[Ultrin]] look up and see the wounded star hanging in the sky. It has a green wound from where it was struck. There is a mist or light bleeding out of it. Around the edges of the wound is rippling, as if burning at the edges. For a moment the glow stops and it looks as if something is shifting within the wound.
	- [[Ultrin]] can see that it looks like a beating heart, with rhythmic pulsing. Thin black threads are emitted from each pulse and descend towards the city, brushing against the barrier and dissipating. Each time the barrier is touched in this way it shimmers, showing some pressure.
	- Every time there is a tolling of a bell the heart pulses.
	- [[Ultrin]] and [[Merric]] are both reminded of what happened to [[The Weave]] And the infinite threads of light being pulled towards the ship, except this is darkness & shadow. It feels as if [[Nihal]] is reaching for something, but the barrier is preventing it, but struggling.
	- The [[Starspawn of Nihal]] swarming the barrier are currently all stationary, which feels wrong.
	- [[Aust]] points out that pack animals will rest when a wounded prey is seriously enough wounded that the battle is won and they can wait for their prey to die.
	- [[Merric]] gets the sense that the mourning bells aligning everyone to think of the same thing has power in it. The tolling is disturbing the star and focusing on the city, but the barrier is also shimmering as if the barrier is being strengthened. It feels as if the bells are helping and hindering.
		- [[Jorund]] knows the tolling is ritualistic, unifying the city and powering the ritual of sending a soul to rest, but that isnt happening here and could potentially be subverted. Also religion isnt working as it should & a city wide ritual is perhaps susceptible to corruption. 
	- After about 10-15 seconds the strands from the star stop. 
	- [[Jorund]] suggests moving up the timetable for the funeral, rather than just stopping the bells which may make the populous afraid. 
- [[Jorund]] talks briefly to a guard and tells him to keep believing. The guard asks what to believe in, and [[Jorund]] tells him to believe in the [[Raven Queen]]. 
	- They chat about how [[Lord Neverember]] will help the party liberate [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]

Based on the session notes, I can see that **Ultrin struck Nihal and created the green wound**, but the notes don't specify the exact session date when this occurred or what it cost him. 

The notes indicate that:
- Ultrin struck Nihal, creating a green wound that now marks her presence in the sky
- The wound appears as an "oozing green lesion that pulses rhythmically like a dying heart"
- However, the damage "has not diminished her power—only made her more dangerous"

The notes also mention that **Ultrin was used as a material component in Archmage Nand's spell** to create the barrier against Nihal, which changed The Weave itself. This may be related to the cost, but the session notes don't explicitly state what price Ultrin paid for wounding Nihal.

The campaign notes don't contain the specific session number and date when Ultrin wounded Nihal, nor do they detail the exact cost to him. Do you have additional information about when this occurred?

Or join those who feel that [[Neverwinter Academy]] is more important than the rest of the city, as only they can fight cosmic evils.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests there are 100s of wizards still in [[Neverwinter Academy]] at varying levels, of which 3 have formed an acting council:
		- [[Professor History]] believes that we should sacrifice [[Neverwinter Academy]]
		- [[Dean Maverin Soul]], master of adjuration, thinks that [[Neverwinter Academy]] should be teleported somewhere and be floating above us.
		- [[Thameor Vex]] is a ritualist, believes that magic shouldn't be as prescriptive as the books say, that magic can be more powerful if we get rid of the guardrails, and this will unlock power to fight out enemies.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] mentions that magic has shifted and spells and enchantments have changed. Most magic is now less powerful.
	- [[Ultrin]] asks why the magic artefacts aren't just taken out of [[Neverwinter Academy]] and why moving it has taken such a following. Apparently walking through the umbilical cord to the city is quite an experience. 
		- Magic that would have previously been quick, easy and predictable that would be useful for moving things - now no longer are
		- The magical rooms could not be used without moving the whole building. 
		- The space for growing crops would also be lost without taking the whole building
		- [[Ultrin]] is against a magical [[Neverwinter Academy]] hovering over the city and thinks it should just land somewhere. [[Ultrin]] suggests that [[Neverwinter Academy]] could be moved to the river, as there's lots of space there, just build a platform first.
	- [[Ultrin]] suggests that a powerful banishment might be able to send [[Nihal]] away. [[Magister Eloin Vale]] asks why [[Nand]] would bring the creature closer, but [[Ultrin]] says that he doesn't believe that was a part of [[Nand]]'s plan and the creature took advantage of things (unless something went wrong).
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] says she will head to [[Neverwinter Academy]] and share what [[Lord Neverember]] has said today. [[Ultrin]] asks [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to prepare for [[Ultrin]] visiting. She says that she has a small research team that is currently investigating [[The Weave]] but perhaps that is irrelevant and irreversible, so they could change to another topic if [[Ultrin]] prefers. 
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for research into mass banishment & mass dispel curse 
		- [[Ultrin]] briefly has a fleeting thought about asking them to research lich spells, but realises this isnt his thought and turns it away.
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for a 500GP jewel for "spell components"
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] also mentions that [[Neverwinter Academy]] will be interested in the rest of the party as "material components" in a spell. [[Ultrin]] suggests that the party probably isnt aware of this and should just be called material witnesses. 
- At this point the doors of the chamber open and the crowd starts to disperse around the city, in a variety of conversations on the way out.
- [[Ultrin]] says to the party that we need to visit [[Neverwinter Academy]] so he can use his influence to do what's right, but [[Ultrin]] ==doesn't know whats right==. 
	- [[Jorund]] uses message to ask [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to bring some seeds for [[Aust]]. She walks over and asks what sort of plants, is told those for growing healing potion reagents & food. [[Aust]] passes over the list from [[Guildmistress Rayleen]]
- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] each take one of the ==war ponies== that the city guard use. [[Aust]] is mildly unimpressed at such use of an animal when they could just walk.
	- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] head to the [[House of Knowledge]]
	- [[Ultrin]] & [[Aust]] head to [[Neverwinter Academy]]
- [[Jorund]] doesn't notice much on the trip. People around are facedown, not looking at the sky. 
	- [[Merric]] and [[Ultrin]] look up and see the wounded star hanging in the sky. It has a green wound from where it was struck. There is a mist or light bleeding out of it. Around the edges of the wound is rippling, as if burning at the edges. For a moment the glow stops and it looks as if something is shifting within the wound.
	- [[Ultrin]] can see that it looks like a beating heart, with rhythmic pulsing. Thin black threads are emitted from each pulse and descend towards the city, brushing against the barrier and dissipating. Each time the barrier is touched in this way it shimmers, showing some pressure.
	- Every time there is a tolling of a bell the heart pulses.
	- [[Ultrin]] and [[Merric]] are both reminded of what happened to [[The Weave]] And the infinite threads of light being pulled towards the ship, except this is darkness & shadow. It feels as if [[Nihal]] is reaching for something, but the barrier is preventing it, but struggling.
	- The [[Starspawn of Nihal]] swarming the barrier are currently all stationary, which feels wrong.
	- [[Aust]] points out that pack animals will rest when a wounded prey is seriously enough wounded that the battle is won and they can wait for their prey to die.
	- [[Merric]] gets the sense that the mourning bells aligning everyone to think of the same thing has power in it. The tolling is disturbing the star and focusing on the city, but the barrier is also shimmering as if the barrier is being strengthened. It feels as if the bells are helping and hindering.
		- [[Jorund]] knows the tolling is ritualistic, unifying the city and powering the ritual of sending a soul to rest, but that isnt happening here and could potentially be subverted. Also religion isnt working as it should & a city wide ritual is perhaps susceptible to corruption. 
	- After about 10-15 seconds the strands from the star stop. 
	- [[Jorund]] suggests moving up the timetable for the funeral, rather than just stopping the bells which may make the populous afraid. 
- [[Jorund]] talks briefly to a guard and tells him to keep believing. The guard asks what to believe in, and [[Jorund]] tells him to believe in the [[Raven Queen]]. 
	- They chat about how [[Lord Neverember]] will help the party liberate [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]

content.text:
Based on the session notes, I can see that **Ultrin struck Nihal and created the green wound**, but the notes don't specify the exact session date when this occurred or what it cost him. 

The notes indicate that:
- Ultrin struck Nihal, creating a green wound that now marks her presence in the sky
- The wound appears as an "oozing green lesion that pulses rhythmically like a dying heart"
- However, the damage "has not diminished her power—only made her more dangerous"

The notes also mention that **Ultrin was used as a material component in Archmage Nand's spell** to create the barrier against Nihal, which changed The Weave itself. This may be related to the cost, but the session notes don't explicitly state what price Ultrin paid for wounding Nihal.

The campaign notes don't contain the specific session number and date when Ultrin wounded Nihal, nor do they detail the exact cost to him. Do you have additional information about when this occurred?

Based on the available information, it is not explicitly stated what had changed before Lord Neverember's proclamation. However, it is mentioned that Lord Neverember seemed genuinely changed, and his speech was candid and unpolished, indicating a shift in his demeanor and approach.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party first became aware of Neverember's potential involvement in the plague investigation when his official seals (or convincing forgeries) were discovered on crates at [[My Chemical Reagents]] containing watered-down healing potions with suspicious egg-like objects. His seal also appeared on orders given to the [[Tarrith]], though the party later learned Tarrith was not directly employed by him and received instructions via dead drops—suggesting his seal may have been forged or misused.

By Session 35-36, Neverember was visibly worn by the crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]], privately confessing the city's desperate state while maintaining a facade of authority. He reconciled with [[Arlon]] and revealed that [[Edgar]] died defending unmarked graves at the party's estate. Most significantly, in Session 37, he convened faction representatives at [[Castle Never]] and delivered an unusually raw, unpolished speech investing the party with his full authority, pledging to personally accompany them to [[Neverdeath Graveyard]].

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

In Session 10, [[Edgar]] revealed that he had rejected numerous bribes but ultimately could not prevent the prisoner [[Tarrith]] from escaping the manor. He subsequently delivered a letter from the [[Circle of 9s]] to the party, serving as an unwilling intermediary in this exchange.

In Session 35, [[Edgar]]'s status became uncertain, and castle staff promised to check the census to verify whether he was still alive or accounted for, suggesting he may have been missing or his fate unclear at that time.

By Session 36, it was confirmed that [[Edgar]] had died defending two unmarked graves somewhere on or near the party's estate. Lord Neverember personally confirmed his death, though the identities of those whose graves he died protecting remain a mystery.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The shop served as a storage facility for mysterious crates bearing [[Lord Neverember]]'s seals, discovered to contain watered-down healing potions mixed with suspicious egg-like objects—items connected to the plague investigation. In a dramatic turn, the establishment was deliberately burned down by a [[Drow]] operative acting under orders from someone bearing [[Lord Neverember]]'s seal, destroying evidence and eliminating the location as an investigation point. The party's assigned watcher failed to prevent or even notice the arson, suggesting either skilled execution or inadequate surveillance.

The party has conducted stakeout operations at the location and recruited assistance from [[Aarlon]] to maintain observation of the premises.
