		- The logbook becomes unhinged In its purchases overtime - underdark hearbs and “delicacies from home”
		- Starts speaking of “the master’s associates” and preparing celebratory feasts for multiple people for the final 2w. Final entry is 5d ago. 
- Another room is a Library. There is furnature facing a small central table strune with books. Spider silk curtains cover the windows. The bookshelves are filled with religious texts for pretty much every god and pantheon. There is a section on [[Lolth]] which is larger than the rest. A glass fronted bookshelf has books on [[Kyransalee]] (with a glyph of warding).
- There is a sitting room with a polished grand piano covered in dust. There are plush pieces of furniture around the fireplace and embroidered with spiderweb patterns.  
	- An expensive looking chess set, the onyx pieces are dark elven figures, the ivory pieces are skeletons. The chess game is setup and midway through game but is also dusty. 
	- The piano keys and the chess set are enchanted.  The piano seems enchanted to play itself under some circumstance. The chess set is enchanted to do something when the game ends.
	- The fireplace mantle has a draw built in. It contains a few vials of dark liquid and a scrawled note in fake script - translates to “to taint The Weave”. The vials have a strong necromantic energy. The liquid seems a magical equivalent of acid as if designed to destroy magic items.
	- Soothing piano music plays when [[Ultrin]] sits on the sofa. It sings the [[Kyransalee]] dedicated Drow ritual “Song of Servitude” as part of binding their souls to her forever. 
- There are main stairs leading upwards. They are less grand than you would expect for a mansion. 
- The final room on the floor is an office, setup with a desk and visitors chair. The room is dishevelled, with draws and cabinets left open, parchment everywhere - almost looks ransacked (by someone who knows it well? So just a disorganised person). 
	- On the desk are diplomatic seals and wax, inkwells and official seals of [[Neverwinter]], the ministry of health and the minister of health and the Silhorn family seal (all seem legit).
	- There is a small glass jar on the desk, containing a pulsating fungel fragment (like in the kitchen).
	- Most of the parchment is official documents of health ministry (various authors). Some stacks of parchment have numbered stacks, missing some pages. Some parchments seem unofficial works - describing people, places, business and rumours about them (no health angle). 
	- There is a locked draw of the desk, undisturbed which looks to have been attempted to be forced, but failed and the lock is now damaged. [[Aust]] mends the lock and. [[Merric]] picks it open.
		- Inside is “Evidence” - a forgery set, some half complete forged documents (identities and job descriptions for recruitment to various organisations). Forged ID for a new recruit to grey cloaks and one for enlistment to Raven Queen inquisition. 
		- There are a few scraps of parchment with references to the party - these are very recent. They mention alligencies and rivalries (our investigation, Arlon, Gerrard Way, Tarrith, the stakeout of My Chemical reagents and visiting the High Inquisitor). 
- Heading up the main stairs, leads to an upper landing. The room is underwhelming, with a tiny chandelier (that is swaying in a non-existent draft) and a suit of armour that feels like its watching us. [[Ultrin]] believes it is enchanted with a trigger to cause it to move. 
- Going up the servant stairs leads to a door that leads onto the same upper landing. There is also a small storage space for servants containing a barrel and a create (containing cleaning and repair supplies). 
- Off the hallway is a bedroom that the party sneaks into ([[Jorund]] staying downstairs). The room is orderly but cramped - clearly for servants. There is a lockbox and a simple desk with a ledger and a small wardrobe opposite the bed. 
	- Opening the lockbox is 15gp, a spare uniform and a personal journal. The journal has personal details of the head servant, details feelings, going from undervalued servant through to more recently “the master” is referred to with disdain, as the master seems obsessed with shadows and whispering in the dark - the servant is uncomfortable and seems to hate the employer. 
	- In the wardrobe is a drawer containing a cracked silver broach with a family crest (Likely a family crest of a house - presumably Drow). 
- [[Merric]] unlocks the next door and opens it. Inside is a trophy room, no furniture other than suit of armour and fireplace. Mounted on the walls are 4 preserved heads - tiefling (horns adorned with gold rings), drawn (beard with iron clasps), human (expression frozen in defiance), male Drow (empty eye sockets carved into symbols of [[Kyransalee]]). The armour seems mundane. 
	- The heads are preserved via necromantic magic. There is a magic glow from the fireplace and the room is cold - coming from the fireplace. 
	- [[Aust]] investigates the fireplace, it seems perfectly normal but appears to contain an invisible cold fire. It seems to be draining warmth from the room. 
	- [[Merric]] investigates the heads (with [[Aust]]’s help). The heads seem to have been hacked off violently, not showing the usual meticulous care for a trophy room. The violence of this is at contrast to their presentation. 
	- The Drow head has something carved into the skin beneath their hair - “betrayer”. The scaring seems to have been done before death. The eye sockets were done post mortem.
- The next room is an alchemy workshop, with a bust of a Drow, upon a pedestal covered in tendril growths. There is an alchemical table with many ingredients. There is another sealed jar of fungel spores which is buckling from the pressure. There is parchment on the table and a sink covered in violet fungi. 
	- The parchment is a stack of research notes on 2 themes - [[aromycos]] and fungus in general, describing it as a destructive force of decay and an opportunity, discussing fungal networks being connected; the other theme is into shadows and shadow magic, binding and harnessing shadows for a greater purpose. There is an attempt to link the 2 - growing fungus in dark places and shadows link the fungal network (seems mad).
	- [[Ultrin]] opens a door and takes a bit of damage from a wave of cold and sharp things lodging in his windpipe. Inside is a cracked urn draining the light around it, with fungal growth from ceiling to floor. 
		- [[Merric]] sees [[Ultrin]] chant something in a morbid rhyme chanting a cantrip that shouldn’t exist. [[Merric]] asks about the spell and [[Ultrin]] says is “Pocket full of poses” a completely normal spell, which [[Merric]] thinks [[Ultrin]] doesn’t seem to entirely believe himself. 
		- The urn seems to be designed to consume light and actively create shadow. It is cracked and cold necromantic energy is pulsing out, as if the urn were bleeding (seems damaged). Everywhere except the urn is covered in mould. This seems to match the research notes.
	- The jar with the fungus on the bench has strangely non uniform growth - it is focused downwards. There is a draw beneath it which [[Merric]] opens and finds a shard of black obsidian which faintly consumes light around it - seems to be a perfect patch for the urn, as if manufactured for this. 
		- [[Merric]] slices the shard in half with several strikes of his scimitar. Dark sparks fly off each time it is struck. The shard seems malevolent, making the urn seem dangerous to smash. 
	- The Drow bust is definitely of a Drow (even without colour in the bust). There is a hidden compartment in the pedestal, which is encircled in white tendrils that he doesn’t want to touch.#Aust #Jorund #Merric #Ultrin #aust #investigation #ivaransylhorn #jorund #merric #neverwinter #ravenqueen #session-23 #soultrial #ultrin #year-2024

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During a critical meeting at the castle, Rayleen brought urgent attention to the severely depleted magical reagent supplies necessary for crafting healing potions. Recognizing the severity of the situation and the need for innovative solutions, she agreed to collaborate on an experimental project: providing seeds to be cultivated by [[Aust]] in the untainted garden at [[Neverwinter Academy]]. This partnership represents a forward-thinking approach to circumvent traditional supply shortages and ensure a sustainable, local source of alchemical ingredients.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

The Sphere of Annihilation operates as an unstoppable force of consumption. Anything drawn into its event horizon is obliterated—whether physical matter or magical energy. The sphere does not hunt or think; it simply exists as a nexus of destruction, consuming whatever falls within its reach.

When [[Merric]] forced the sphere into the [[Starspawn]], it became a focal point for [[Nand]]'s forbidden ritual. The sphere's interior became a conduit for otherworldly visions, displaying imagery of nature, earth, and the [[Shadowfell]]—suggesting it can serve as a gateway or lens through which other realms bleed through. This revealed the sphere's potential utility for purposes beyond mere annihilation, though such applications remain deeply dangerous and poorly understood.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

The Sphere of Annihilation is a devastating magical phenomenon—a roiling void of absolute destruction that consumes everything in its path. Rather than a creature with agency, it functions as a weapon of immense power, capable of obliterating matter and magic alike. During the [[Plague Investigation]] arc, the party encountered this sphere as it became the centerpiece of a catastrophic magical working.

The sphere's true nature remains partially mysterious, though its manifestation suggests it taps into fundamental forces of unmakings and the void between worlds. Its appearance as a swirling darkness that devours light and substance makes it both terrifying and difficult to comprehend through normal sensory perception.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

[[Dawnbringer]] is a sentient divine weapon carried by [[Jorund]]. She communicates with him through his mind and possesses a distinct personality marked by deep emotional sensitivity and theological concerns. Her nature as a divine artifact suggests a connection to the celestial or godly realms, though the exact nature of her divinity remains unclear.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The Academy operates largely outside the main magical barrier protecting [[Neverwinter]], though the barrier has been stretched to provide marginal protection. Access is gained via an unstable magical tunnel that distorts the Weave and reality itself—magical runes light up to strengthen the barrier as travelers pass through. The institution contains specialized magical rooms and crop-growing facilities that are permanently integrated into the structure and cannot be relocated without moving the entire building.

The Academy maintains advanced runic ward-work capabilities, crafting protective glyphs for vessels that allow them to float above waves. Scholars within practice sophisticated magical disciplines, and the institution historically grew magical reagents, though these have begun dying in recent years.

retrieved.heading: Notable NPCs
retrieved.document:
## Notable NPCs

The facility is operated by a woman bearing a [[Zhentarim]] tattoo. She maintains a deliberately hands-off approach to her business operations, cooperating minimally with local authorities and asking few questions of her clients. Her willingness to overlook suspicious activity and her organizational affiliation suggest the warehouse may serve as a discreet storage option for those preferring anonymity and minimal oversight.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Iyldan borrowed restricted recipes from the [[Whispering Vials]], which he subsequently returned—though evidence of erasures in the ledger suggests attempts to obscure these transactions. [[Ultrin]] is aware of stories concerning his dangerous alchemical work, lending credence to concerns about what he may have been studying or attempting to create.

retrieved.heading: Abilities & Behaviour
retrieved.document:
## Abilities & Behaviour

The [[Drow]] servants derive their primary power from synchronization with nearby magical urns. When these urns pulse in rhythm with a heartbeat, the warriors become engulfed in shadow energy, gaining significant empowerment and enhanced combat capabilities. This dependency makes them formidable only when the urns are active.

Once separated from their power source—specifically after the urns were destroyed—the servants lost their dark energy manifestations and reverted to baseline drow combat abilities. Despite this loss of magical augmentation, they demonstrated tactical coordination and aggression, particularly during the final confrontation where they swarmed and focused fire on [[Ultrin]].

Their behavior suggests some degree of coordination or command structure, as they coordinated their assault rather than acting as individual warriors. The loss of shadow empowerment did not cause them to flee or surrender, indicating either unwavering loyalty to their master or magical compulsion keeping them in service.

## Headed to [[Raven’s nest]]

- This is close to the Dock district, buildings are less fancy and more industrial here. 
- The building is a plain large warehouse, there are many wooden stairs on the outside leading to the many levels. There are likely 100s of units inside. 
- [[Aust]] starts screaming at a [[Seagull]] and then offers it some food and speaks with it. 
	- He asks the seagull to find out if [[Ivaran Sylhorn]] has been using the building often
- Inside the building there is a human woman, a huge ledger and many lock boxes with wooden doors.
- [[Jorund]] casts invisibility on [[Merric]] and then the two of them plus [[Ultrin]] head inside. 
	- [[Jorund]] asks about renting a room and the different sizes. He asks to see some units. 
	- The woman has a crescent moon/black dragon tattoo - which is a symbol of the [[Zhentarim]]. She starts showing off the rooms.
- [[Seagull]] returns to [[Aust]] and tells him that the man has been seen at the warehouse. There have been many “shinyies” being brought and taken away from the warehouse. “Trinkets, Baubles”. 
	- People often turn up at dusk. Some people with swords enter, others go past. One person with a sword is a friend of [[Seagull]]. He is a protector of the building. 
	- [[Merric]] snaps a lock pick in the lock and takes a while to get the lock open. He then opens the lock. Inside is a think and well organised ledger with a list of storage units on each page and the customers who were rented the unit and if the unit is currently taken.
	- [[Merric]] checks the 30x30 rooms and finds no interesting names. 
	- [[Jorund]] starts talking security. She explains it’s not super high security. There are 2 copies of the keys to each locker and there is no cooperation with the authorities (unless forced) - in which case they attempt to inform the customer.
	- [[Jorund]] asks about insurance the fire risk for the wooden room. She starts walking off.
	- [[Ultrin]] casts suggestion to force her to show them both a locker on the top floor. 
		- They are shown a unit. 60GP per the day. No long term discount. 
		- If the fees aren’t payed there is an auction run at the trade house (on a slow day). The locks get changed. 
		- They are told that there have not been any break-ins for some time.
		- Sometimes people get lost.
	- [[Merric]] finds [[Ivaran Sylhorn]] is renting unit 17C (a 10x10). This reservation is long term (9-10months) compared to most others (much shorter). ==Suspiciously long.==
		- Find other shop proprietors, [[Jerrard|Jerrard Way]], various others. They are all short term and closed.
		- A neighbouring unit - 17B is rented out to Mr Anders has been for 40days, still in for another 20days.
		- [[Merric]] makes a mould from the 17C key and the master key.
	- [[Jorund]] agrees to rent the unit they viewed for a 10day. 352A
	- [[Jorund]] and [[Ultrin]] head to the [[Broken Cask]] pub. Get some ales and some food (various protein pie)
	- [[Merric]] and [[Aust]] head in search of the locker.  [[Aust]] finds the way, despite [[Merric]]’s best attempts. They find 17C. There is no lighting inside the unit. 
		- The unit is somewhat full of various boxes and creates - they are smeared in grime and covered in dust, they are not well maintained and don’t look to have been accessed. 
		- There are shelves filled with alchemy reagents and some old dusty scrolls. 
		- There is a faint unsettling smell of mildew/damp/sickly. (The rest of the facility smelled of dry wood). The floor is stained with dark splotches in some places. These have a viscous liquid texture to them, like something that seeped into it. Could have been some kind of blood? Hard to identify given the age. OR could be a spilled concoction/ichor. 
		- At the end of the unit are a number of dusty cotton sacks strewn around and full of stuff. These are very dusty, but less than the creates.
		- Poking the sacks with a stick shows they are stuffed with various misshapen things. They appear to be stuffed with many different things. Some bits are soft, others are hard. 
		- The contents are a bag of stuff that looks fit for charity donation - ornaments, clothing, rugs, blankets. It feels like they are setup to look like storing. 
		- There is a false flooring beneath the flooring. Moving this reveals an intricately carved wooden box - the carvings look like potentially magical runes. 
		- [[Aust]] uses detect magic and can see the carvings are glowing and there are hidden carvings on the floor of the compartment with conjugation magic. The floor around the box also seems warded.
		- [[Aust]] casts dispel magic which fails. [[Merric]] inspects the runes again and can see the intention is if the box is moved then something will be conjured. This also triggers on opening the box. There is also abyssal script about the binding of shadows - suggests a shadowy beast could be conjured. The wards look powerful. 
		- [[Aust]] successfully dispels the wards on the box and floor on a 2nd attempt at a higher level. [[Merric]] takes a rubbing of the etching on the box and floor and then unlocks the box (after [[Aust]] failed to open it).
		- Inside the box is lined with lead. The only thing inside is a key which has a faint aura of necromantic magic. ==It has a small emerald set into it.== 
		- There is a very thin hidden compartment in the bottom of the box. [[Merric]] breaks the box slightly to find a piece of folded parchment, it seems to be a magical blueprint for a magical artefact. It seems like a first attempt at working out an artefact but they don’t understand how. It is incomplete, so it’s hard to see what they’re creating. It seems to be a sphere and there are spirals of runes within the sphere. There are measurements on the drawing indicating it’s palm sized. There are references to “forces of destruction”. 
	- [[Merric]] inspects the other objects in the room. They seem generally in keeping with the role of a health minister - ointments, antidotes, etc. Legit healing reagents. But no health potions.#Aust #Jorund #Merric #Ultrin #aust #ivaransylhorn #jorund #merric #session-20 #ultrin #year-2024

## At [[Castle Never]]

- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] approached [[Ultrin]]. This was someone who joined the [[Neverwinter Academy]] around the same time as [[Ultrin]], but followed a more normal path, working more closely with politicians. She is used to hiding her feelings, but [[Ultrin]] can see her studying him
	- "I had started preparing myself to hear your name at the funeral, yet here you stand, returned. I am glad I was wrong. You have made yourself interesting and important."
	- "You will be aware by now that [[Neverwinter Academy]] has no head, and unfortunately for you [[Nand]] saw something in you and entrusted everything to you. The academy is split, but they will all look to you and try to convince you they are right. You may have to decide who is right"
	-  [[Ultrin]] asks who she favours. She says what is being done to the barrier should be impossible, only after [[Nand|Archmage Nandntiersanon]]'s spell did it become possible, and [[Ultrin]] was the material component of the spell. 
	- She asks if [[Ultrin]] knows why the weave has changed. He says that he isn't sure, he hasn't done the research. Claims you can see [[The Weave]]  in the same way you can see light and that [[Nand]] has changed [[The Weave]], in an attempt to block [[Nihal]], although in turn [[Nihal]] used the alignment of the plains to its own advantage. 
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests that [[Ultrin]] could help those that seek to "finish" the work of [[Nand]]. Or join those that wish to abandon the [[Neverwinter Academy]] and allow the barrier to stop manipulating [[The Weave]]. Or join those who feel that [[Neverwinter Academy]] is more important than the rest of the city, as only they can fight cosmic evils.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] suggests there are 100s of wizards still in [[Neverwinter Academy]] at varying levels, of which 3 have formed an acting council:
		- [[Professor History]] believes that we should sacrifice [[Neverwinter Academy]]
		- [[Dean Maverin Soul]], master of adjuration, thinks that [[Neverwinter Academy]] should be teleported somewhere and be floating above us.
		- [[Thameor Vex]] is a ritualist, believes that magic shouldn't be as prescriptive as the books say, that magic can be more powerful if we get rid of the guardrails, and this will unlock power to fight out enemies.
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] mentions that magic has shifted and spells and enchantments have changed. Most magic is now less powerful.
	- [[Ultrin]] asks why the magic artefacts aren't just taken out of [[Neverwinter Academy]] and why moving it has taken such a following. Apparently walking through the umbilical cord to the city is quite an experience. 
		- Magic that would have previously been quick, easy and predictable that would be useful for moving things - now no longer are
		- The magical rooms could not be used without moving the whole building. 
		- The space for growing crops would also be lost without taking the whole building
		- [[Ultrin]] is against a magical [[Neverwinter Academy]] hovering over the city and thinks it should just land somewhere. [[Ultrin]] suggests that [[Neverwinter Academy]] could be moved to the river, as there's lots of space there, just build a platform first.
	- [[Ultrin]] suggests that a powerful banishment might be able to send [[Nihal]] away. [[Magister Eloin Vale]] asks why [[Nand]] would bring the creature closer, but [[Ultrin]] says that he doesn't believe that was a part of [[Nand]]'s plan and the creature took advantage of things (unless something went wrong).
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] says she will head to [[Neverwinter Academy]] and share what [[Lord Neverember]] has said today. [[Ultrin]] asks [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to prepare for [[Ultrin]] visiting. She says that she has a small research team that is currently investigating [[The Weave]] but perhaps that is irrelevant and irreversible, so they could change to another topic if [[Ultrin]] prefers. 
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for research into mass banishment & mass dispel curse 
		- [[Ultrin]] briefly has a fleeting thought about asking them to research lich spells, but realises this isnt his thought and turns it away.
		- [[Ultrin]] asks for a 500GP jewel for "spell components"
	- [[Magister Eloin Vale]] also mentions that [[Neverwinter Academy]] will be interested in the rest of the party as "material components" in a spell. [[Ultrin]] suggests that the party probably isnt aware of this and should just be called material witnesses. 
- At this point the doors of the chamber open and the crowd starts to disperse around the city, in a variety of conversations on the way out.
- [[Ultrin]] says to the party that we need to visit [[Neverwinter Academy]] so he can use his influence to do what's right, but [[Ultrin]] ==doesn't know whats right==. 
	- [[Jorund]] uses message to ask [[Magister Eloin Vale]] to bring some seeds for [[Aust]]. She walks over and asks what sort of plants, is told those for growing healing potion reagents & food. [[Aust]] passes over the list from [[Guildmistress Rayleen]]
- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] each take one of the ==war ponies== that the city guard use. [[Aust]] is mildly unimpressed at such use of an animal when they could just walk.
	- [[Merric]] & [[Jorund]] head to the [[House of Knowledge]]
	- [[Ultrin]] & [[Aust]] head to [[Neverwinter Academy]]
- [[Jorund]] doesn't notice much on the trip. People around are facedown, not looking at the sky. 
	- [[Merric]] and [[Ultrin]] look up and see the wounded star hanging in the sky. It has a green wound from where it was struck. There is a mist or light bleeding out of it. Around the edges of the wound is rippling, as if burning at the edges. For a moment the glow stops and it looks as if something is shifting within the wound.
	- [[Ultrin]] can see that it looks like a beating heart, with rhythmic pulsing. Thin black threads are emitted from each pulse and descend towards the city, brushing against the barrier and dissipating. Each time the barrier is touched in this way it shimmers, showing some pressure.
	- Every time there is a tolling of a bell the heart pulses.
	- [[Ultrin]] and [[Merric]] are both reminded of what happened to [[The Weave]] And the infinite threads of light being pulled towards the ship, except this is darkness & shadow. It feels as if [[Nihal]] is reaching for something, but the barrier is preventing it, but struggling.
	- The [[Starspawn of Nihal]] swarming the barrier are currently all stationary, which feels wrong.
	- [[Aust]] points out that pack animals will rest when a wounded prey is seriously enough wounded that the battle is won and they can wait for their prey to die.
	- [[Merric]] gets the sense that the mourning bells aligning everyone to think of the same thing has power in it. The tolling is disturbing the star and focusing on the city, but the barrier is also shimmering as if the barrier is being strengthened. It feels as if the bells are helping and hindering.
		- [[Jorund]] knows the tolling is ritualistic, unifying the city and powering the ritual of sending a soul to rest, but that isnt happening here and could potentially be subverted. Also religion isnt working as it should & a city wide ritual is perhaps susceptible to corruption. 
	- After about 10-15 seconds the strands from the star stop. 
	- [[Jorund]] suggests moving up the timetable for the funeral, rather than just stopping the bells which may make the populous afraid. 
- [[Jorund]] talks briefly to a guard and tells him to keep believing. The guard asks what to believe in, and [[Jorund]] tells him to believe in the [[Raven Queen]]. 
	- They chat about how [[Lord Neverember]] will help the party liberate [[Neverdeath Graveyard]]

retrieved.heading: 🎲 Recap
retrieved.document:
## 🎲 Recap

Ultrin convinced each party member to join the final ritual against the worms, claiming they were instruments of fate guided by gods. With everyone assembled, Nand sacrificed himself to activate the spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to Ultrin. As the ritual reached its climax, a massive worm eruption tore through the ship's deck, spawning undead creatures and a pulsating heart. The party fought desperately while Ultrin struggled to control the spell, ultimately consulting the forbidden book to redirect the power at the worm star. The spell succeeded catastrophically—the star retreated and vanished, but the ship plummeted from the Far Realm back toward a drastically changed Neverwinter, crashing into the corrupted river.

retrieved.heading: Access & Contact
retrieved.document:
## Access & Contact

The Fraternity of Order is not easily accessible through direct means. Instead, they can be reached through intermediaries or contacts who have established relationships with the organization. The party has been informed that consultation with the Fraternity is possible contingent upon successful completion of their current mission, suggesting the organization is selective about whom they advise and may require proof of capability or trustworthiness before sharing their knowledge.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The warehouse presents a utilitarian structure typical of dock-district commerce, with weathered wooden stairs providing the primary means of vertical access. The facility's layout accommodates numerous individual units across multiple stories, suggesting substantial square footage and capable storage capacity. The building operates with notably loose security measures, allowing relatively easy access to the premises compared to more regulated establishments.

retrieved.heading: Encounters
retrieved.document:
## Encounters

Initial encounters during the [[Plague Investigation]] proved manageable, with individual specimens being contained. Later incursions in [[Neverwinter]] revealed their true scale and lethality. Military engagements require 20-30 guardsmen to kill a single creature, with casualty rates approaching 50%. The death of Neverwinter's old Minister of Defence during combat with these entities highlighted their lethal capacity. Current siege conditions show the Starspawn maintaining patient vigil around the city's barrier, their stillness suggesting absolute confidence in ultimate victory through attrition and the city's eventual collapse.

retrieved.heading: Description
retrieved.document:
## Description

The manor is accessible via the sewers, suggesting it has underground connections to Neverwinter's broader infrastructure. The location is guarded, with at least two armored sentries posted outside to control access and prevent unauthorized entry.

retrieved.heading: Encounters
retrieved.document:
## Encounters

The Starspawn encountered during the party's [[Plague Investigation]] proved to be a multi-round combat threat requiring sustained effort and coordination to overcome. Its ability to teleport freely made it difficult to pin down, while its spellcasting forced the party to remain tactically vigilant. The creature's vulnerability to divine channeling proved instrumental in the party's eventual victory.

retrieved.document:
## Key Events

During the investigation into corrupted funerary practices, Sister Maelin confirmed that the [[Raven Queen]] did not willingly enable or sanction the corrupted rites that had been discovered. She identified approximately 2-3 cracked mausoleums in a rough area of [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] as locations of concern. She also made the practical decision to defer the funeral of [[Nand]] until after the party's planned mission to the graveyard, explaining that tolling funeral bells prevent spirits from dispersing to other planes—a consideration that affects both spiritual protocol and tactical timing.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Nand's most critical act was sacrificing himself during a ritual against worms at a ship's prow in Session 34. He activated a spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to [[Ultrin]] before collapsing, fundamentally weakening himself in the process. Shortly thereafter, he disappeared while fighting a powerful star spawn, and his body was never recovered. His death triggered formal mourning throughout [[Neverwinter]], though investigations subsequently revealed he had committed crimes. The barrier currently protecting [[Neverwinter]] was created by Nand's spell, leaving the [[Neverwinter Academy]] dependent on his continued presence and guidance.

## 🎲 Recap

The party was hauled from the river by frightened fishermen before [[Kieran]], a cleric of the [[Raven Queen]], dispersed the crowd and escorted them through a grieving [[Neverwinter]] — where black ribbons hung from every door and mourning bells tolled for the presumed death of [[Nand]]. [[Kieran]] revealed that the city's magical barrier had contracted, souls could no longer pass on, and the dead were beginning to "curdle." The party were installed in the royal guest wing of a battered [[Castle Never]], where a castle official briefed them on the state of governance and promised to investigate the whereabouts of [[Edgar]] and [[Arlon]]. Overnight, each party member found their magical items weakened or altered, while [[Aust]]'s courtyard meditation revealed a vast, ancient consciousness pressing against nature — an effect that sharpened with every toll of the mourning bells.

Nand functioned as both mentor and mysterious manipulator throughout the campaign. More ominously, Nand appeared to orchestrate events behind the scenes, arranging rituals and encouraging the party down specific paths. His true agenda—whether benevolent or self-serving—remains unclear.

answer:
Based on the session notes, here's how Nand's funeral plan has shifted and why timing matters:

## The Shift in Plans

**Original Plan:** Nand's funeral was initially set to proceed with formal mourning bells tolling throughout Neverwinter (as seen in Session 35, where black ribbons hung from every door and mourning bells tolled for his presumed death).

**New Plan:** Sister Maelin made the practical decision to **defer Nand's funeral until after the party's planned mission to Neverdeath Graveyard**.

## Why Timing Matters

The timing is critical for two interconnected reasons:

1. **Spiritual Protocol:** Tolling funeral bells prevent spirits from dispersing to other planes. This is a fundamental consideration of the Raven Queen's funerary practices.

2. **Tactical Consequences:** The continuous tolling of mourning bells has been causing severe magical and spiritual disturbances in Neverwinter. According to Session 35, the bells sharpen "a vast, ancient consciousness pressing against nature," and the city's magical barrier has contracted while souls can no longer pass on—they're beginning to "curdle."

By deferring the funeral until after the party investigates the corrupted mausoleums in Neverdeath Graveyard, Sister Maelin is essentially buying time to address the underlying corruption in the graveyard before conducting the full funeral rites. This prevents the bells from continuing to destabilize the city's spiritual and magical state while the party works to resolve the root cause of the corruption.

retrieved.heading: Overview
retrieved.document:
## Overview

Nand was a scholar and figure of authority at the [[Neverwinter Academy]], deeply associated with the [[House of Knowledge]]. He possessed extensive knowledge of arcane matters, forbidden artifacts, and planar lore, serving as a key intellectual resource for the city of [[Neverwinter]]. His expertise encompassed dangerous subjects including evil artifacts, forbidden texts, and otherworldly entities. By the campaign's progression, Nand revealed himself to be far more than a mere academic—a powerful practitioner of weave-magic with the ability to manipulate reality-altering rituals.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

Following the death of [[Archmage Nand]], the Academy has been paralyzed by indecision and endless meetings. Three competing factions now vie for leadership, disagreeing fundamentally on solutions to the institutional crisis: whether to relocate the Academy, preserve forbidden knowledge, or focus on fighting the broader crisis affecting [[Neverwinter]]. Information-sharing with the [[House of Knowledge]] has decreased due to fear of disseminating dangerous knowledge.

The Academy was identified as a target for destruction by an entity in [[Merric]]'s dream during plague investigations. It collaborates with local druids on water purification crystal maintenance, funded by the [[Council of Neverwinter]].

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party has repeatedly sought specific maps from the House of Knowledge for their investigations. [[Nand]] was unable to access a crucial map showing the hidden island location of [[Sangrullo]]'s lair during an early visit. Maps pertaining to [[Wormcrawl]] and information about the necromancer [[Kyusse]] are also housed here. More recently, the institution began systematically redacting and relocating books dealing with divine ascension and corrupted rites, suggesting heightened concern about particular forbidden knowledge. The House has committed to providing blueprints of [[Neverdeath Graveyard]] once specific crypts are identified by the party.

retrieved.heading: Key Events
retrieved.document:
## Key Events

The party was transported to the Farm Realm through [[Nand]]'s planar alignment spell during their campaign against the worm infestation. On the deck of their ship, they assembled a spell circle at the prow and conducted a final ritual designed to combat the worms and [[Nihal]]. The ritual provoked a violent eruption from the worm star, causing catastrophic disturbances throughout the immediate area.

After this confrontation, the creatures of the Farm Realm demonstrated awareness of the party's presence. When [[Ultrin]] opened his shutters, he witnessed hideous entities from beyond the realm's barrier turning to look directly at him, indicating that the creatures possess some form of sentience and interest in the intruders from the material plane.

retrieved.heading: 🎲 Recap
retrieved.document:
## 🎲 Recap

Ultrin convinced each party member to join the final ritual against the worms, claiming they were instruments of fate guided by gods. With everyone assembled, Nand sacrificed himself to activate the spell circle, transferring incomprehensible weave-power to Ultrin. As the ritual reached its climax, a massive worm eruption tore through the ship's deck, spawning undead creatures and a pulsating heart. The party fought desperately while Ultrin struggled to control the spell, ultimately consulting the forbidden book to redirect the power at the worm star. The spell succeeded catastrophically—the star retreated and vanished, but the ship plummeted from the Far Realm back toward a drastically changed Neverwinter, crashing into the corrupted river.

retrieved.heading: Corruption and Destabilization
retrieved.document:
## Corruption and Destabilization

[[Nihal]]'s solar flare pulses are actively consuming the Weave, threatening to unravel all magical structure and cause the gods themselves to fade from existence. This consumption has rendered the Weave vulnerable to external manipulation and has fundamentally weakened magic throughout reality.

Following [[Nand]]'s ritual sacrifice during the events at [[Neverwinter]], the Weave was catastrophically altered. An incomprehensible magical force was channeled through Nand's death and concentrated upon [[Ultrin]], transforming the fundamental nature of magic itself. The threads of the Weave became temporarily tangible, feeding into a sphere of energy that enveloped Ultrin like a shawl, granting him infinite magical power.

The consequences have been severe: magic throughout the world is now significantly less powerful than before, with spells and enchantments fundamentally changed. The tunnel to [[Neverwinter Academy]] experiences particularly volatile magical instability, making the Weave unpredictable and dangerous to those who channel it.

retrieved.heading: 🧵 Open Threads
retrieved.document:
## 🧵 Open Threads

- Did Nand survive sacrificing himself to power the ritual?
- What is the extent of Ultrin's new connection to The Weave after absorbing its remnants?
- Has the book gained a stronger hold on Ultrin after he used it to redirect the spell?
- What happened to Neverwinter while the party was in the Far Realm—why is the city partially in darkness with the barrier shrunk?
- What are the "incomprehensible shapes" moving beyond the city walls?
- Why are Castle Never's banners at half-mast?
- Did the party survive the crash into the corrupted river?
